programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Aiden try to solve the murder of a rap artist, who was stabbed after signing a recording contract and winning a DJ contest. Meanwhile, Stella and Danny search for the killer of a fashion designer found in her penthouse pool - a case that leads them to a sushi restaurant specialising in food served on nude models. With hip-hop star Master P

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Danny investigate the case of a building worker who died in an underground explosion. An X-ray reveals about 200 of the victim's bones are broken and the autopsy shows he succumbed to asthmatic asphyxiation before the blast. Meanwhile, Stella and Aiden examine the death of a teenager found strangled and floating in a creek

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Danny and Aiden investigate the case of employees of a Brooklyn cafe found dead with plastic bags over their heads. As events unfold, a discovery is made about the type of duct tape used on the victims which could help track down the killer. Meanwhile, Mac and Stella set out to locate the body of a man whose severed lower leg has turned up in an alleyway

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team is asked to investigate when three convicts break out of prison, including a brilliant hacker who was forced to help the other criminals steal billions of dollars online. Meanwhile, Paige struggles to manage Ralph's expectations when his father returns. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: A musical prodigy assists Scorpion in investigating the murder of his friend, who was a music industry blogger. Meanwhile, Drew takes Ralph to a baseball game and later visits the headquarters to seek Walter's advice in a bid to help him connect with his son. Drama, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A fire crew discovers a man's body after putting out a raging blaze at a warehouse belonging to a Navy-contracted electronics company, and Gibbs recognises the victim as Carter Plimpton - a former member of an NCIS black-ops unit. Meanwhile, a member of the Baltimore police department helping with the case appears to have a grudge against Tony

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team's mission to track the arsonist targeting US Navy facilities appears to end in success when the man in question is killed by a thermite explosion on board the USS Brewer. Meanwhile, Abby studies evidence relating to fire-starter Bruce Johnson, who died when his car exploded, and discovers coded messages in his quarters that indicate he may have been working as part of a larger gang. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The investigators discover probationary agent Ned Dorneget has been bugged by Harper Dearing, the man behind a spate of arson attacks on US Navy vessels. A phone call from the man himself makes it clear he is out for revenge, and Sam Ryan has information as to the motive behind it. Guest starring Richard Schiff (The West Wing)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team tries to track down wanted terrorist Harper Dearing before another US Navy ship is destroyed, and a desperate Gibbs enlists the aid of a prisoner to draw his target out of hiding. Meanwhile, Palmer is forced to consider putting his nuptials on hold as the stand-off with Dearing becomes heated. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The aftermath of Harper Dearing's bomb attack on the NCIS headquarters dominates proceedings. As rescue crews sift through the debris searching for survivors, Abby, Gibbs, McGee and Vance are accounted for, but Ziva and Tony are trapped in a lift. Dearing makes contact after his ex-wife is taken into custody, enabling his car to be traced to an abandoned lot - and the agents arrive just in time to see the vehicle blow up. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.desc: Deeks is forced to resume an old alias he hates in a bid to protect his best friend and chief informant from arms traffickers who want him dead. Crime drama, starring Eric Christian Olsen, Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team goes in search of a stolen radiotherapy canister that could have been used to make a bomb after a man impersonating a Navy Seal dies after being set on fire. Meanwhile, team leader Hetty Lange has been acting strangely and Callen offers to help with whatever is troubling her. Guest starring Claire Forlani (Camelot), with Chris O'Donnell and Linda Hunt

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen and the team are perplexed by Hetty's sudden resignation and press Vance and their new team leader Lauren Hunter for more information about what happened to Hetty. Visiting one of her homes for clues, they find mercenaries ransacking the place - and try to track down the man who hired them. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Agents G Callen and Sam Hanna investigate after a naval officer is killed during an LAPD shootout, with two other men who died in the gun battle being identified as members of a Mexican drug cartel. Spin-off drama following the exploits of the Los Angeles-based Office of Special Projects, an elite NCIS division specialising in undercover assignments. Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Three men stage a raid on a drug deal, taking a suitcase full of cash and killing the dealer and an undercover LAPD officer. While studying footage taken from CCTV equipment in the house, Sam recognises the distinctive hand signals of one of the suspects as being those of Navy Seal Kurt Holgate, who he served with in Bosnia. Eric studies the details of his unit and finds that a man called Jackson has been unaccounted for since the day of the attack. Drama, starring LL Cool J

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A gang member is shot and his dying words in the ambulance are the name of corrupt ex-policeman Ray Moore. Heaton struggles to find evidence linking the victim with his arch enemy, but Lewis's contacts reveal the red scorpion tag sprayed on all of Moore's properties was the dead man's handiwork. Phil's first day back in plain clothes gets off to a bad start

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Lewis's girlfriend nurse Tash Niles is attacked in the hospital car park, raising the terrifying prospect that Ray Moore is targeting people who could testify against him. Diane encounters a pensioner who turns out to be a fan of Reg's podcast Regflections - and clearly has other guilty secrets to hide

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A road accident victim (former Hear'Say singer Suzanne Shaw) is found alone in the wreck of her car, but Crime Scene Examiner Lorna Hart is convinced a second person was in the vehicle at the time of the accident. As Rudy's condition deteriorates, Tash finally agrees to testify against Ray Moore, but the case isn't moving fast enough for Lewis, who takes matters into his own hands

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: As Lewis's fingerprints are found all over the gun next to Ray Moore's body, the young PC heads for the station to make a statement and hand in his warrant card, while Heaton regrets losing the chance to bring his old enemy to justice. New recruit DC Grace Dasari (This Life's Amita Dhiri) teams up with Mickey to help a woman trace her son, who has been missing for 11 years

programme.title: Les Misérables
programme.desc: Adaptation of Victor Hugo's novel, which follows an epic journey through 19th-century France as the nation stands on the brink of revolution. Dominic West plays the lead role of Jean Valjean, a former convict attempting to begin his life anew while all around him a desperate underclass struggles to survive. Valjean's search for a fresh start is thwarted by prejudice against ex-convicts and the suspicion of an obsessed former guard who is convinced he will reoffend. Elsewhere, an army officer returns home from the battle of Waterloo desperate to reconnect with his son, while a seamstress has an encounter that could lead to her downfall. With David Oyelowo, Lily Collins and David Bradley

programme.title: Les Misérables
programme.desc: A desperately poor Fantine is forced to flee Paris in search of work, and is left contemplating a terrible decision that could ensure her daughter enjoys a brighter future. Her fate takes an even darker turn as she struggles to meet the increasingly outrageous demands of the Thenardiers. Valjean settles into his new life as a prosperous factory owner, but his newfound happiness is threatened when Javert is appointed as the town's new police inspector, and remains as determined as ever to expose him

programme.title: Les Misérables
programme.desc: Jean Valjean is tormented by his conscience and, under relentless pressure from Javert, is forced to confess his true identity. However, his life looks is changed entirely once again due to a promise he makes to Fantine to keep Cosette safe. The Thenardiers see a bright future ahead when a wealthy stranger seeks lodgings at their inn - but their joy is doomed to be short-lived. Javert rises to become a police officer in Paris, and pursues a new lead involving a report of a missing girl that could finally allow him to bring Valjean down

programme.title: Les Misérables
programme.desc: Cosette has grown into a teenager, and to Valjean's dismay, questions her closeted life and is desperate for a new start in Paris. The opportunity presents itself when she meets Pontmercy's son Marius, who has turned his back on his family after learning the truth about his father, and now lives in a squalid tenement, where he catches the eye of the daughter of a criminal family. Javert ascends through the ranks of the police, and believes he is closing in on his goal

programme.title: Les Misérables
programme.desc: Paris stands on the brink of revolt during the funeral of General Lamarque. Enjolras leads a group of protesting students and workers to construct a barricade in the streets, while an increasingly desperate Valjean fears for Cosette's safety and arranges for her to go into hiding as Javert's search for them grows ever more relentless. Marius scours the city for the missing Cosette, assisted by a heartbroken Eponine - but when he cannot find her, he is left consumed with despair and decides to join Enjolras at the forefront of the uprising

programme.title: Les Misérables
programme.desc: The army prepares to make another attack on the barricades as the fighting grows more vicious and desperate. Valjean feels conflicted about Marius' relationship with Cosette and faces a heartbreaking choice when he comes to realise what is in her best interests. However, he underestimates the strength of Cosette's feelings for him. Javert is thrown into turmoil by the selfless actions of a man he has always viewed as an irredeemable criminal, and is forced to reconsider everything he believes in

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Alvin dresses as a crow, but finds it difficult to walk in a straight line, and the disguise gives Howard an idea as he tries to hide from Pearl. Long-running comedy, starring Peter Sallis, Frank Thornton, Brian Murphy and Robert Fyfe

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The Roman Catholic priest sets out to protect an old friend by investigating a 300-year-old secret hidden in a university. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Boxing Day sees heavy snowfall covering the country and the adverse weather causes major disruption across Poplar as roads become blocked and pipes freeze meaning families have to queue outside for water from standpipes. Sister Julienne must inform a family about the death of their loved one and uncovers an uncomfortable truth, while Valerie helps an unmarried couple living in a caravan prepare for their baby, but unforeseen complications knock her confidence in her ability as a midwife. Guest starring Anita Dobson

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: First of a two-part story by Lynda La Plante in which the team investigate the death of a prostitute who plunged from the balcony of a block of luxury apartments. They discover she was the daughter of a famous Seventies TV star murdered 12 years previously, whose killer was never traced, and try to find a link between the two crimes. David Hayman stars as the newly promoted DCS Michael Walker, alongside Victoria Smurfit, Colin Salmon and Frances Barber

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Concluding part. Jonathan Southwood is taken in for questioning, during which a series of shocking revelations point to the true murderer. Walker faces a race against time to reach Connor, who is working undercover at a private club where Colin Thorpe is putting her professionalism to the ultimate test. Crime drama, starring Victoria Smurfit, David Hayman and Nicholas Farrell

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Henry VIII is desperate to end his marriage to Katherine of Aragon, but Cardinal Wolsey's efforts to persuade the pope to grant an annulment are fruitless - much to the dismay of Thomas Cromwell, a lawyer dependent on Wolsey's patronage. The king's new mistress is rumoured to be Anne Boleyn, who has sworn vengeance on Wolsey after he refused to let her marry Harry Percy, so Cromwell visits her in the hope of swaying her to the cardinal's side. Historical drama adapted by Peter Straughan from Hilary Mantel's Booker Prize-winning novels Wolf Hall and Bring Up the Bodies, and starring Mark Rylance, Damian Lewis, Claire Foy and Jonathan Pryce

programme.title: Birds of a Feather

Comedy, with Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.desc: A dying patient who Maria helps reunite with her mother offers the injured nurse renewed hope of walking again. Connie impresses the hospital's new chief executive with her surgical skills, while Chrissie is buoyed by the discovery she is able to get pregnant and searches for a man. Leslie Ash joins the cast as the CEO Vanessa Lytton

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Comfort is asked to identify a body, and desperately hopes it will not be Fin. Abs is startled when Ellen suggests how he could help her stay in the country, while Bex's concern for Luke prompts her to pay him a visit - only to discover he's allowed Kate to move in

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Sleeplessness and a taxing workload catch up with Jack, who is lucky to escape from a road accident so bizarre his colleagues begin to suspect he is losing the plot. His protege Mickey Webb sets out to rescue his boss's reputation and save his career. An explosion in a lock-up on an industrial estate alerts Neil to possible terrorist activity in the manor

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: A mysterious phone call gets Alan and Carol worried about Robbie. Ricky gives Pat a piece of his mind and Kathy is surprised when she talks to Frank

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Robbie and Tony have something to celebrate, while Tiffany cannot share Bianca's enthusiasm for work. Pauline is stood up and Phil's worst fears are confirmed when he visits Frank

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: Military drama series charting the lives of the men and women of the King's Own Fusiliers regiment. In the opening episode, the officers return from a tour in Northern Ireland after tragedy strikes. Starring Robson Green and Jerome Flynn

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: Detective Inspector Henry Crabbe prepares for his retirement and the opening of his restaurant - but the police force seems reluctant to let him go. First episode of the crime drama, starring Richard Griffiths and Maggie Steed

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James and Helen get ready to move to Rowangarth and experience more of Granville Bennett's hospitality. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Lynda Bellingham

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: First episode of the comedy. The death of her husband leaves upper-class snob Audrey fforbes-Hamilton a widow in drastically reduced circumstances, with her beloved ancestral pile up for auction. Starring Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Pilot episode of the vintage comedy. Trouble abounds when the Ladies' Department and Gentlemen's Outfitting are informed they must share the same floor. John Inman, Mollie Sugden, Frank Thornton and Wendy Richard star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Smiler takes up dancing to impress the woman of his dreams and recruits Truly to help accomplish the task. Comedy, starring Stephen Lewis and Frank Thornton

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Howard is embarrassed when he bumps into an acquaintance from his past, and Truly's bid to help him backfires. Long-running comedy, starring Robert Fyfe, Peter Sallis and Frank Thornton

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The Roman Catholic priest struggles to save Sid from himself when he vows vengeance on his former defence barrister. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: DI Mooney and the team become embroiled in the mysterious world of faith healing when a blind woman dies while being treated by renowned healer Steadman King. When it turns out the victim was poisoned, all eyes turn to Steadman and his cup of holy water. But how could the prime suspect have carried out the deed in front of an audience without anyone noticing? Meanwhile, JP is put through his paces when Pico the police dog joins the team on a week-long trial, and Florence tries to recreate some of Jack's home comforts

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Sandra loses her trust in the others when she discovers they have withheld information concerning her father. The revelation he committed suicide while under investigation for corruption causes her to doubt everything she took for granted, but she tries to set her feelings aside when asked to look into the death of a circus ringmaster

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. DS Boyd is approached by a woman convinced her father's death in a car crash was not an accident or suicide, but something far more sinister. Intrigued by the evidence she has collected, he agrees to re-open the case, and with the help of forensic scientist Dr Frankie Wharton, investigates a third option not previously considered. Trevor Eve and Holly Aird star

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Anne Boleyn summons Cromwell, anxious to know whether she has an ally in him, and he witnesses her venting her frustrations on her ladies-in-waiting. The king starts to take notice of Cromwell and comes to rely on the lawyer's advice, but then news arrives from York that Cardinal Wolsey has been arrested by Harry Percy, a vengeful act for denying his betrothal to Anne eight years earlier. Historical drama adapted from Hilary Mantel's Booker prize-winning novels Wolf Hall and Bring Up the Bodies, starring Mark Rylance, Damian Lewis, Claire Foy and Jonathan Pryce

A blunder by Abs leads to a patient having a cardiac arrest, but Ellen's efforts to win him over by covering for his mistake fail miserably, and Sam is left seething when he is rejected by Bex after taking her out for a drink

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Leela and Tony discover the body of a supermodel who had recently fronted an anti-fur campaign despite her lack of real conviction. Lorna notes that the crime scene has been arranged to resemble a shot from the film, suggesting animal rights activists may have resented her hypocrisy. Subsequent investigations reveal a host of rival male admirers with reason to kill her and Terry digs up evidence of a more sinister side to the woman's life

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil turns to Pat in desperation. Pauline feels guilty about a telephone call and Blossom takes Tiffany to task

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Frank has another breakdown as Phil watches every move he makes. Alan receives bad news and Pat's visitor proves too much for her

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: Major Tom Cadman discovers that his wife has been having an affair with a rival officer whilst he has been in Norther Ireland

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: Henry is enlisted in the search for a missing policeman, but the restaurant's opening night is looming. Detective drama, starring Richard Griffiths and Maggie Steed

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Siegfried gives another lecture on economy to Calum, who is visited by Molly McFeely, with whose family he stayed while in Ireland - while Deirdre also pays a call at the same time. Meanwhile, James receives an invitation to examine Lord Buttermere's new stallion. Rural drama, starring John McGlynn, Robert Hardy and Christopher Timothy

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: Audrey moves out of Grantleigh Manor, and Richard and his mother move in, disappointing the former resident with their new ways. Comedy, starring Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: A romantic invitation from Mr Lucas to Miss Brahms is delivered to Mrs Slocombe by mistake. Shop-floor comedy, with Trevor Bannister, Mollie Sugden and Wendy Richard. First broadcast in 1973

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: A man foolishly believes a curse has been placed on him, so Truly, Entwistle and Alvin feel it is their duty to perform an exorcism. Peter Sallis and Frank Thornton star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Barry becomes dejected when his fitness-mad neighbour Boothroyd begins to outshine him in all things physical. However, he discovers that when it comes to karate, he may just have an advantage over his new rival. Long-running comedy, guest starring Brian Conley

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Radio quiz Up to You comes to Kembleford and the sleuth suspects one of the cast is responsible when the landlady of the house they are staying at is murdered. Mark Williams stars

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: It seems somebody has taken the island's Day of the Dead celebrations a little too literally when a man receives a voicemail from his wife saying she is being attacked. When she turns up dead, suspicion falls upon her friends and acquaintances at the nearby yacht club - her husband Finn, club secretary Charlotte and chairman Hugh. However, Jack's curiosity is piqued by a yellow butterfly found on the victim's clothing and he calls upon Commissioner Selwyn's knowledge of such matters. Caribbean whodunit, guest starring John Sessions and Pippa Haywood

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Ricky Hanson faces trial for attempted murder, and his lawyer sets about trying to discredit each member of the squad. However, Jack receives a welcome distraction when an old friend asks him to reinvestigate the death of a wealthy businessman, and he, Brian and Gerry spring into action. Guest starring David Troughton, with Amanda Redman, James Bolam, Alun Armstrong and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: When a skull is found buried in concrete in a church crypt, Boyd seeks to uncover who it belongs to and why it is there, but gets little help from the Church authorities, who seem focused on obscuring the facts. The discovery of another body, adorned with ritualistic symbols, leads the team to a suspicious workman and an uncooperative retired priest. Trevor Eve and Sue Johnston star in a feature-length episode, with guest appearances by Annette Crosbie and Barry Morse

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Cromwell manoeuvres a bill through Parliament acknowledging Henry as head of the Church of England, the first step in enabling the king to grant his own divorce from Katherine of Aragon. The Boleyn family also looks to the lawyer to deal with Harry Percy, whose wife claims her own marriage is unlawful on the grounds that her husband had previously made a binding contract of marriage with Anne Boleyn. This is a task Cromwell approaches with relish, remembering how Percy helped bring down Wolsey. Historical drama, starring Mark Rylance, Damian Lewis and Claire Foy

programme.title: Sunday Worship

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits a large two-bedroom apartment in Battersea, south-west London, while Martin Roberts looks over plans to develop a house in Winsford, Cheshire

programme.title: Planes: Fire & Rescue (2014)
programme.desc: A racing plane suffers damage to his engine, and realises he can never compete again. He starts a new career as a member of a firefighting squadron, which proves even more dangerous than he expected when the aircraft have to deal with a massive forest blaze. Animated adventure sequel, with the voices of Dane Cook and Ed Harris

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross oversees oversees proceedings at the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells as two teams scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell on for profit. Experts Philip Serrell and Thomas Plant attempt to guide the sides to success at auction in Cardiff

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins explores St Paul's Cathedral, with music including hymns sung by the Cathedral Choir and performances by Aled Jones and the Priests, and Daniel O'Donnell

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton heads to the Welsh Borders to help a couple with a budget of £775,000 find a countryside home to raise their young son and establish a glamping retreat business

programme.title: Pete's Dragon (2016)
programme.desc: Ranger Grace finds an orphaned 10-year-old boy in the forest, where he has been ever since the death of his parents five years earlier. What's more, he tells her he has been living with a giant, fire-breathing dragon. Could her father's tales of a mythical beast be true after all? Fantasy adventure, starring Bryce Dallas Howard, Robert Redford and Oakes Fegley

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The team are in Leamington Spa to help Robert and Gemma and their daughter Darcey, who is afraid of the family's nettle-strewn, overgrown garden

programme.title: Penguins: Meet the Family
programme.desc: Documentary following penguins living in unexpected environments, including satellite imagery of previously undiscovered colonies of flightless birds. Among their habitats are dense forests, desert burrows and city streets. The programme reveals the challenges they face in making their homes in these challenging environments, from evading predators to scaling rocks, and captures the lifelong bonds formed between family groups

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher looks back at 2020, a difficult year when the countryside became a lifeline for unprecedented numbers during the pandemic, while wildlife made their homes in curious places during lockdown. Sean also catches up with young people at the forefront of the battle to protect the environment

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The programme returns with Fiona Bruce and the team visiting Forty Hall in Enfield, north London, where items brought in for assessment include a set of Star Wars toys, and a policeman's helmet worn by John Lennon. There is also a rock crystal Faberge cigarette case complete with a ruby, an early post-box, and a poster for one of Pink Floyd's very first concerts

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a documentary revealing how the forces of nature drive, shape and support the Earth's great diversity of life. The first edition examines volcanoes, which responsible for both for the planet's breathable atmosphere and the oceans, but are also the architects of the planet, with over 80% of the Earth's surface being the result of magma bursting up from the molten interior - providing a platform for life

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: Marie-Andree Leclerc falls under the spell of Charles Sobhraj while on holiday in India and is persuaded to move to Bangkok to live with him, but he does not give her the life he promised he. When Charles does takes Marie-Andrée to the beach, he enlists her to befriend an Australian couple, who he drugs and robs of their money and passports. Drama, starring Tahar Rahim and Jenna Coleman

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the latest Premier League action, featuring Chelsea v Manchester City, Burnley v Fulham and Newcastle United v Leicester City. Neither Chelsea nor Manchester City have found the consistency yet this season to suggest that winning the title is within their grasp, but victory against their rivals here would send out a statement to the rest of the challengers

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: The second of two festive specials of the comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll. Winnie has not been sleeping well, and wants to tell Agnes about her dreams, so when her house is later burgled and her mother's wedding ring stolen, Buster and Dermot plan to take matters into their own hands to expose the thief. Cathy has a significant birthday coming up - and is far from happy about it

programme.title: Escape from Alcatraz (1979)
programme.desc: A cunning convict with a history of making escape attempts is transferred to the notorious high-security island prison off the California coast, from which no one has ever broken out. Continually at odds with the brutal warden, he and three fellow prisoners hatch a plan to make it to freedom on an improvised raft. Thriller, based on a true story, starring Clint Eastwood, Patrick McGoohan, Roberts Blossom and Fred Ward

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Dan Walker and Louise Minchin

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville return to examine more disastrous holidays and travel stories, beginning by hearing from passengers whose dream cruise was ruined by a fire on board - which meant missing out the highlight of the trip

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the progress of a semi-detached in Farnworth near Bolton, a one-bedroom apartment in Trent Vale, Stoke-on-Trent, and a listed building in Maidstone, Kent

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: After leaving her native South Africa when her young daughter became critically ill, Zelease Ballentine Brain felt her young children needed to live in a place where being outside in the sunshine was the norm. Husband Matt was naturally cautious, and needed to see that Australia would give the family all that Zelease had talked about. Could a trial week swing him round to her way of thinking and, three years on, where have they decided to settle? Presented by Nicki Chapman

programme.title: Moment of Proof
programme.desc: The stories of pivotal moments in police investigations when detectives realise they have cracked a case, told through interviews and dramatic reconstructions. In the first edition, a single text message - hidden among thousands - nails a serial fraudster, and an extraordinary surveillance operation leads straight to the kidnappers and their hostage

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Richard Madley and Stephanie Connell help the teams look for items at the Brackley Antiques Cellar in Northamptonshire, while Eric Knowles learns about the art of signwriting

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Lady Felicia and Mrs McCarthy are taken hostage when a midnight train robbery goes awry. Crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Sorcha Cusack and Nancy Carroll

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers share nostalgic dishes that conjure up memories of childhood, holidays and bygone days, preparing a Lancashire hotpot just like Dave's mother used to make

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A health-conscious couple from London enlist Sonali Shah to help them spend £1.3million on a detached property in Surrey with enough land to create a wellness retreat

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: The return of the programme celebrating inspirational farming families and the rural events where they showcase their hard work. Sheep and arable farmers John and Ed head to Moseley Farmers' Market, but when they arrive their pitch location threatens to put a dent in their earnings. The Styan family, meanwhile, need to sell as much veg as possible for cash to make some much-needed repairs to their windblown farm

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers returns with the programme in which five dealers bid against one another to buy intriguing items brought in by members of the public. The opening edition features a tribal mask, avintage seed drill, adecorative press, a toy horse and a mysterious Edwardian piece

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas host the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville creates a special blend of snuff, but will his customers notice any beneficial effects? The ladies get dressed up for line-dancing, while Gastric is prompted to go on a diet. A furtive Mr Newbold thinks he's found a pastime to share with Mrs Rossi - away from the prying eyes of Mrs Featherstone. However, a determined Black Widow enlists assistance from Granville and Leroy for a crash-course in how to ride a bicycle. David Jason, Stephanie Cole, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Maggie Ollerenshaw star

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A desperate Stacey turns on Ruby as her concern for Kush worsens. Martin defends his wife, and when Ruby tries ringing, things are made even worse when Arthur answers. Callum's luck has run out as Jack delivers bad news and the cracks start to show as Sonia struggles to cope at

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Ben finds out that all Janey's suitors are part-time teachers and Susan discovers that employing Nick is not for the faint-hearted. Comedy, starring Robert Lindsay, Zoe Wanamaker, Daniela Denby-Ashe and Kris Marshall

programme.title: Traces
programme.desc: Emma Hedges' return to Dundee to start a new job brings her mother's unsolved murder into sharp focus as she fights to bring the killer to justice. However, before she makes the journey back to her home town, a fatal fire in a nightclub proves ominous. Crime drama by Amelia Bullmore, based on an original idea by Val McDermid, starring Molly Windsor, Laura Fraser, Martin Compston and Jennifer Spence

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: While appearing on a chat show discussing a year in lockdown, Michael and David are pained to discover someone they respect is not a fan of their work. David's intention to go to work and Michael's plans to visit a friend abroad are both scuppered by last-minute travel rule changes. The return of the comedy, starring David Tennant and Michael Sheen

programme.title: Accused: A Mother on Trial
programme.desc: Documentary following the case of Alaskan woman Ashley Ard as she faces the prospect of up to 99 years in prison for murdering her newborn child. Her lawyer Rex Lamont-Butler compiles the defence with the help of local private detective Tank Jones, who revisits the park where the baby was abandoned, convinced that Ashley is not who the public think she is

programme.title: The Science(ish) of Stranger Things
programme.desc: Comedian Sophie Duker, presenter Rick Edwards and physicist Michael Brooks dissect the science behind the hit Netflix sci-fi series, sorting the nanoscience from the nonsense

programme.title: The Wall Versus Celebrities
programme.desc: Danny Dyer hosts this second special celebrity edition of the game show, which sees Last Leg presenter Alex Brooker and actress Sally Lindsay teaming up to win as much cash as possible for their chosen charities. But their chances rest on the unpredictability of the Wall and their unflinching trust in one another

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Dan Walker and Louise Minchin

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: Stories of holiday-makers left stranded at the airport because their Covid-19 test results came back too late, and people who arrived at a hotel only to discover it was closed

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit two neighbouring cottages in Derbyshire, a terrace in Bolton and a semi in Leeds before they are sold under the hammer

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Claire Duffy wanted the best in life for her young family, especially youngest daughter Abbie, who had recently received a cystic fibrosis diagnosis. She was sure that the warm weather and outdoor lifestyle of New Zealand would do wonders for Abbie's health, but had to convince home bird husband Russell that moving halfway across the world was a viable option. Two years later, have they made the leap, or was the pull of home just too strong? Nicki Chapman catches up with the family to find out

programme.title: Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A pair of pyjamas reveal the shocking true identity of an attacker who left his victim for dead, and a bright yellow baseball cap puts a daylight robber behind bars

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Caroline Hawley and Roo Irvine help two teams look for items in an antiques centre in the Lancashire town of Eccleston. Eric Knowles overseeing proceedings and also attempts a spot of Morris Dancing, discovering the history of the humble clog and the part it played in Lancashire's industrial heritage

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A new musical director for the bell-ringers of St Mary's leads to discord, disharmony and the blood-curdling sound of murder. Mystery, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers cook comfort food dishes straight from the oven, including a new take on steak and chips and sticky gingerbread

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a house hunter from Surrey to find a new home in Pembrokeshire, and finds her a remote period house with spectacular views within her budget of £900,000

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Hexham's fortnightly market brings hope of selling some lamb and beef to farmer Andrew and his daughter Emma, and Lee and Shelly are selling produce from their Tamworth pigs

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents the programme in which five dealers bid against one another to buy a pedal car, an Edwardian card table, a quirky voice box and a mystery pot

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Jack sets his plan in motion and manages to negotiate an advantageous deal, meaning he soon has good news for Denise. Stacey is in for a surprise, Whitney has a choice to make and Ruby has to come up with an explanation for Martin. Ash has advice for Sonia after she ends another exhausting shift at the hospital

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Sacha buries his head in the sand when Beka is taken ill on New Year's Eve. Kian is on edge as Sarah Jane avoids his calls, and his stash is running out. He tries to convince Jac he is fine when she worries that their relationship is the problem. It is total chaos on AAU, and the F1s are daunted when Hanssen leaves them alone to face their first New Year's night shift

programme.title: The Crash Detectives
programme.desc: Behind the scenes with Gwent Police's dedicated forensic collision investigators as they deal with serious incidents on the roads. In this edition, a hit-and-run leaves a cyclist fighting for her life, while mystery surrounds the death of a delivery driver on a busy dual carriageway

programme.title: Traces
programme.desc: As Kathy and Sarah work on their reports for the Secrets nightclub fire, the emerging picture is that two of the deaths were accidental, but the third victim was dead before the fire started. Sarah's report has some damning things to say about the safety of the building, but soon feeling overwhelmed by the police interest she wonders what she's started. Emma is cautioned by DI McKinven to stop sleuthing, as the stars align to put Daniel Macafee in her path - for whom she feels a deep connection. It later transpires that Daniel harbours a guilty conscience as he was employed by the company that refurbished Secrets. Crime drama by Amelia Bullmore, starring Molly Windsor, Laura Fraser and Jennifer Spence

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: After discovering the network's plan and learning they will not be going to America, David and Michael are dismayed to learn who will be going in their place. The guys are also upset to find out how well Simon is doing without them

programme.title: Accused: A Mother on Trial
programme.desc: Rex searches for evidence to implicate Kennard in the crime. Details of Ashley and Kennard's past reveal why she might be covering up for him. Kennard finally breaks his silence

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: Contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing a cash prize. Helping them this time round are Stacey Dooley, Roman Kemp, Omid Djalili, Judy Murray, Owain Wyn Evans, Kate Humble and Vikkstar

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter V, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison meets Simon King in Bristol to get a crash course in how to photograph wildlife. They attempt to capture on camera birds in flight over the Avon Gorge, inquisitive city squirrels, and a herd of deer who live semi-wild in the nearby Ashton Court Estate. Plus, a look through Countryfile's archive for some of Ellie's wildlife highlights

programme.title: Glorious Gardens from Above
programme.desc: Horticulturist Christine Walkden takes to the skies above Sussex in a hot-air balloon. She drops in at the gardens of West Dean, where she witnesses the fruits of a 20-year labour of love, and marvels at the transformation of a former car park into an Italian landscape at Arundel Castle. Plus, the contribution the Romans made to British gardening

programme.title: Life in a Cottage Garden with Carol Klein
programme.desc: In September and October, the horticulturist harvests seeds and pricks out young plants to aid growth. She also prepares the garden for visitors to her Open Garden Scheme day, and enlists her daughters' help to decide the future of a cankered apple tree

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Gardens
programme.desc: Monty Don continues his horticultural tour with a visit to the dreamy climes of Australia and New Zealand. Visiting the renowned Botany Bay, Monty investigates the effect imported plant-life, favoured by colonial settlers, has had on the natural beauty of New South Wales. In New Zealand, the green-fingered presenter goes in search of the wild heart of the country, revelling in a lush rainforest conservatory made entirely of native plants

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Simple Suppers
programme.desc: The food writer makes mouth-watering chocolate cookies and crispy duck with honey and figs, which prove a little bit of what you fancy does you good

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Sylvia Syms narrates a history of Westerns, featuring archive interviews with stars including Gary Cooper, James Stewart, Yul Brynner, Paul Newman, John Wayne and Clint Eastwood

programme.title: How the West Was Won (1962)
programme.desc: Three generations of a pioneering family experience many ordeals and adventures in their endeavour to build a new life for themselves on the American frontier in the 19th century. Over the years, the family experiences the key events of the nation's history, including the gold rush, the Civil War, and the building of the railroads. Epic Western, starring James Stewart, John Wayne, Gregory Peck, Henry Fonda, Debbie Reynolds, Lee J Cobb and Richard Widmark

programme.title: Wild West: America's Great Frontier
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the history and geography of America's deserts, canyons and wilderness, exploring the lives of the people and animals that have found ways to thrive and prosper in these brutally inhospitable conditions. The first programme examines the agricultural traditions of the Hopi Native American tribe in northeastern Arizona, as well as exploring the strategies used by a variety of wildlife, including mustangs, coyotes and desert tortoises, to survive in these demanding environments

programme.title: The Culture Show
programme.desc: Saving Mr Banks charts the troubled production of 1964 children's classic Mary Poppins, which was based on the series of books by PL Travers. In this programme, Victoria Coren Mitchell explores the dark and complex life of the writer, revealing her 20-year battle with Walt Disney and how the big-screen adaptation overshadowed her writings but made her rich. With contributions by Emma Thompson and Cameron Mackintosh

programme.title: Saving Mr Banks (2013)
programme.desc: Walt Disney becomes determined to adapt Mary Poppins into a film, but faces an obstacle in the shape of author PL Travers, who resists the idea of a sentimental Hollywood version of her book. As the movie mogul's charm offensive comes unstuck, he begins to realise why the story is so important to its creator. Fact-based drama, with Emma Thompson, Tom Hanks, Paul Giamatti and Colin Farrell

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: Documentary about the Cornish fishing village of Mevagissey. While many fishing communities are struggling, this one has thrived owing to protection from prevailing winds and returning fish stocks. Boats have been handed down through generations and currently 74 operate out of the harbour. As young captains prepare to follow in their father's footsteps, tourism also becomes a major boost to the town - but leaves it feeling deserted in winter. A veteran skipper prepares to retire, and questions what the next generation will learn from his example

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Jenny Campbell and Tej Lalvani assess the profit-making potential of ideas for flour-free bread and hair extension storage, as well as an online business proposition from would-be entrepreneurs in the US. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: Last Woman on Earth with Sara Pascoe
programme.desc: The comedian's world tour of unusual jobs takes her to Georgia, a country bordered by Russia and the Black Sea that has gone through a drastic period of modernisation in the last 10 years - leaving behind some very rare trades with centuries of history. Among the last practitioners of distinctive Georgian skills are a gunsmith, a polyphonic singer and a guide at the Stalin Museum

programme.title: A Teacher
programme.desc: English teacher Claire Wilson arrives for her first term at Westerbrook High full of enthusiasm. Dissatisfied in her marriage, she finds herself attracted to one of her students, and considers an inappropriate relationship that could have crushing consequences for them both. Drama, starring Kate Mara

programme.title: A Teacher
programme.desc: When a party at Logan's house is broken up by the cops, Eric asks for a favour from Claire, who agrees to help, privately knowing that sharing this secret will bring the two of them closer together. Claire struggles to be honest with herself about it, but she is moving Eric closer to crossing a boundary she knows they should not cross

programme.title: Miriam Margolyes: Almost Australian
programme.desc: The Oxford-born actress, who became an Australian citizen six years ago, embarks on a 10,000km drive around the country discovering more about the place she can now call home. Starting in Bondi, she meets up with an estate agent who reveals the prosperous migrant market. Heading west, Miriam is disturbed to discover that there is a growing number of women who can't afford a home in retirement, now making up the fastest growing number of homeless in the country. With most of eastern Australia in the grip of severe drought, Miriam heads to the country town of Trundle, to see the repercussions for herself

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: After a pre-emptive strike against Hiro and Ando in Africa, Arthur orders Knox and Flint to hunt down his son Nathan and grand-daughter Claire. At Pinehearst, Sylar has a charged confrontation with Elle, while Suresh begins testing his new, combined super-power formula. With the others dead, injured or on the run, Matt tries to wake Angela

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Part one of two. After reuniting Sylar and Elle, Arthur orders the pair to bring Claire to him. Matt, Hiro and Ando follow Daphne to her home in Georgia to discover what she has been hiding. As an eclipse plays havoc with everyone's powers, Peter and Nathan travel to Haiti in search of the only man capable of stopping their father from destroying the world - the Haitian. Guest starring Seth Green (Family Guy)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Doctor is locked away in a high-security alien prison. Isolated, alone, with no hope of escape. Far away, on Earth, her best friends, Yaz, Ryan and Graham, have to pick up their lives without her. However, it is not easy as old habits die hard - especially when they discover a disturbing plan forming. A plan that involves a Dalek. Sci-fi adventure, starring Jodie Whittaker, Bradley Walsh and John Barrowman

programme.title: Remarkable Places to Eat
programme.desc: Michel Roux Jr takes Fred Sirieix for a Christmas treat in his favourite British city, Bristol. First up is a French bistro where they enjoy a stunning cote de boeuf and Michel makes aligot, known as the best mashed potato in the world. The duo then enjoy a swim in one of Britain's oldest lidos, which has an award-winning restaurant serving up a delicious breakfast. Escaping to the countryside, they visit a countryside hotel and restaurant when Fred makes buffalo mozzarella while Michel samples the hotel's own cider, before a return to the city and the multicultural neighbourhood of St Pauls, where they learn to cook Caribbean jerk turkey

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp, Nick Hall and Raj Bisram are in Newbury, where Natasha gets to meet a local museum curator

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Adam Partridge and Christina Trevanion value objects at Hereford Cathedral, while presenter Paul Martin visits a local green wood studio

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits the biggest cider factory in the world, which produces more than 350 million litres each year. Cherry Healey explores how the factory is creating new orchards of a sweet apple variety called Scrumptious. And historian Ruth Goodman traces a Victorian apple breeding boom with Dr Annie Gray, recreating one of Queen Victoria's favourite baked apple desserts

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Businessman Mish, facilities manager Victoria and financial services consultant Simon choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves. Quiz, hosted by Rob Beckett

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: Antiques expert Roo Irvine, trader Danny Sebastian and furniture designer Nessa Doran O'Reilly head to West Kilbride to search for bargains

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Christina Trevanion search for items at a West Sussex car boot sale with a budget of £250 each, with Mark battling broken bones to make a profit

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott serves up a family-sized slab of chocolate recipes from the BBC archives, guaranteed to please the nation's chocoholics. The menu includes the Greedy Italians' classic chocolate pudding, the Hairy Bikers 'world's best' brownies, a chocolate mousse from Raymond Blanc, James Martin's marshmallows with chocolate dipping sauce and a surprise from Nigella Lawson - chocolate pasta

programme.title: Made in Great Britain
programme.desc: Steph McGovern tells the story of how the nation's craft and manufacturing skills have shaped its towns and cities and built modern Britain, taking four craftspeople on a journey around the country to discover what made the UK great. They begin by examining Sheffield's rapid transformation from a rural market town to an industrial powerhouse known as Steel City

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Mozart's situation improves after she is let back into the gang, but the Whiskers suffer a tragedy when they battle with the Commandos

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Burton Constable Hall in the East Riding of Yorkshire, antiques expert Catherine Southon enthuses about a diamond ring and Charles Hanson enjoys trying on a pair of boxing gloves signed by some of the greats of the boxing world. Presenter Paul Martin discovers the skeleton of a 58-foot sperm whale, which was washed up on a nearby beach in Victorian times

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Gemma Cairney, Tim Key, Gabby Logan and Jeff Stelling go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: The chef reveals the Cornwall that he knows and loves, championing the food, history, music, art and culture of the county,. He begins at his family home in Trevose Head on the north coast, where as a child he would go fishing with his father

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: The chef and a group of volunteers from Gloucester embark on an eight-week weight loss and fitness journey, doing regular exercise and eating low-calorie meals in an effort to get in shape and reach their health and fitness goals. In the first episode, Tom calls the group to a sports faculty at a local university to reveal what they have signed up to for the next two months

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contestants to brave the black chair, asking them questions on the specialist subjects of Life on Mars, the Titanic, Barbara Castle, and the Periodic Table, before they are tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts another second-round match as the Apres Skiers and the Polyhymnians compete for a place in the quarter-finals. The contestants must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of Upsilon particles, N-rays, Polywater, and Cold fusion

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second round of the student quiz concludes, with two teams of four pitting their wits against one another as they attempt to secure the last place in the quarter-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: MasterChef judge Gregg Wallace and mathematician Hannah Fry take over a restaurant and invite five celebrities to enjoy a dinner party with a difference, where they will be scored on the carbon footprint of every dish they choose. Diners Sara Pascoe, Amol Rajan, Nikki Fox, Desiree Burch and Matthew Fort choose from a menu of tantalising treats, each of which tells its own environmental story

programme.title: A Teacher
programme.desc: Eric has asked his sweetheart Alison Miller to the prom, and the friends have a big night planned. But with Claire working as one of the staff overseers for the night, Eric feels compelled to confront her, and soon there will be no turning back

programme.title: A Teacher
programme.desc: Eric is confused, distracted and passionately trying to be alone with Claire again, while she commits herself to a path that may destroy them both

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events

programme.title: Celebrity: A 21st Century Story
programme.desc: The world of celebrity has been transformed since the turn of the century, driven by some of the most dramatic technological and cultural developments in recent times. The first episode examines how the Beckhams changed the model of the celebrity power couple, while the rise of reality TV led to a gigantic influx of people gaining overnight fame, fed by a press that saw an easy source of material. The programme also examines how leaked sex tapes changed the nature of celebrity scandal

programme.title: Celebrity: A 21st Century Story
programme.desc: Charting the story of celebrity in the late noughties, the second episode looks at how different groups cashed in on the public's insatiable appetite for access to the famous. Young celebrities were commoditised, criticised and discarded by the people who profited from them, until a new breed of celebrity emerged, ready to wrestle control back for themselves. As the demand for the public to have access to their idols' every move increased, celebrity magazine circulation exploded, and celebrities found themselves up for scrutiny by a new type of showbiz journalism

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison meets Simon King in Bristol to get a crash course in how to photograph wildlife. They attempt to capture on camera birds in flight over the Avon Gorge, inquisitive city squirrels, and a herd of deer who live semi-wild in the nearby Ashton Court Estate. Plus, a look through Countryfile's archive for some of Ellie's wildlife highlights

programme.title: Anton Ferdinand: Football, Racism & Me
programme.desc: The former professional footballer goes on a deeply personal journey to explore the issue of racial abuse in the game, talking about his own highly-publicised 2011 incident with ex-England captain John Terry. Anton confronts how the ripple effects of the incident and the online abuse he experienced not only affected his own mental health and career but also the lives of his loved ones. He reveals where he believes players are still being failed by the system, and the ways in which football urgently needs to change

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: After leaving her native South Africa when her young daughter became critically ill, Zelease Ballentine Brain felt her young children needed to live in a place where being outside in the sunshine was the norm. Husband Matt was naturally cautious, and needed to see that Australia would give the family all that Zelease had talked about. Could a trial week swing him round to her way of thinking and, three years on, where have they decided to settle? Presented by Nicki Chapman

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: The return of the programme celebrating inspirational farming families and the rural events where they showcase their hard work. Sheep and arable farmers John and Ed head to Moseley Farmers' Market, but when they arrive their pitch location threatens to put a dent in their earnings. The Styan family, meanwhile, need to sell as much veg as possible for cash to make some much-needed repairs to their windblown farm

programme.title: Nadiya's American Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain continues her culinary journey with a visit to California, home to one of the most diverse populations in America. She is invited to a Mexican quinceanera - an extravagant fiesta celebrating a girl's 15th birthday - cooks for an all-woman daredevil rodeo riding team and makes handmade fortune cookies with a grandma in one of the last remaining workshops in San Francisco's Chinatown

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Rob Beckett hosts the quiz in which contestants must choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves

programme.title: Coast and Country Auctions
programme.desc: The stories behind Britain's traditional markets, following auctioneers, buyers and sellers, beginning in Devon at Brixham fish auction. Stephen Tompkinson narrates

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion and Mark Stacey are off to Belgium, where a Victorian cane topper and an acupuncture model catch their eye, but it remains to be seen which is the most valuable

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of recipes from the BBC archives for party food, including Lorraine Pascale's white chocolate lollipops, seafood dishes by James Martin and Rachel Khoo's coq-au-vin on a stick

programme.title: Made in Great Britain
programme.desc: Steph McGovern pays a visit to York, where chocolate was transformed from a luxury drink for the rich into the nation's favourite treat. She meets up with Yorkshireman and chocolatier Paul A Young, who guides her through the history of the city's association with the food. Chocolate started as an expensive luxury drink, until the manufacture of a solid product became possible. Initially, hand-decorated chocolates with exotic flavours were sold to wealthy customers before, in the 1930s, chocolate became available for all as in the form of the Kit Kat bar

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Flower's pups find themselves alone during a storm when their uncle Youssarian abandons his babysitting duties. Meanwhile, the Lazulis try to expand their territory and move a little too close for the Whiskers' liking

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques experts Caroline Hawley and Michael Baggott find objects at the University of Nottingham to take off to auction. Caroline comes across a jacket worn by Nottingham-born Olympic ice skater Jayne Torvill, and Michael gets excited by a 17th-century Bible that is hiding a secret. Host Paul Martin travels to north Nottinghamshire to visit the Portland Collection, which is one of the largest aristocratic art collections in Britain

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Gemma Cairney, Tim Key, Gabby Logan and Jeff Stelling go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick meets a family who make some of the best Gouda cheese in Britain, and uses their creations to make a tortilla dish with caramelised apples and onions. In the fishing village of Mevagissey, he discovers the origins of the sea shanty and the history of the pilchard fishing industry, when shoals of pilchards were so large they would attract killer whales

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: Competition is fierce as the ring-netters of Newlyn go on the hunt for sardines. Also known as pilchards, they were once a staple of the Cornish fishing industry, caught using drift nets by the tens-of-thousands, then packed in salt and sent to Italy. But demand waned and the drift net fishery, once the mainstay of the Cornish fleet, faded away in the 1960s. In recent years, the humble pilchard has been rebranded as the Cornish sardine, and the hundreds of drift netters have been replaced by just 15 hi-tech ring-netters - and the sardine is once more a highly-prized and lucrative catch

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a sock factory in Leicester that produces one and a half million socks annually, while Cherry Healey discovers the science behind smelly feet and tests brands of socks to see how they cope with the problem. Ruth Goodman looks back at how socks became a fashion statement in the 1980s and a vital piece of military kit in the First World War

programme.title: Cheetah Family & Me
programme.desc: Part one of two. Wildlife film-maker Gordon Buchanan gets close to wild cheetah in the Kalahari, experiencing first-hand the very real dangers these beautiful big cats face. The programme follows two females, each with a litter of cubs, as they navigate a harsh environment, dangerous wildlife and dwindling prey

programme.title: A Teacher
programme.desc: For Eric's 18th birthday, Claire takes him away in secret for the weekend. Away from his friends and family when he should be with them, Eric is blissfully unaware of just how far down a path of destruction he has been taken. Later, Claire makes a decision that will finally bring her crashing into reality

programme.title: A Teacher
programme.desc: With their secret out, Eric has to contend with the whole school knowing and Claire is forced reluctantly to confront the reality of what she has done. With neither of them thinking straight, a bad situation quickly takes a turn for the worse

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events

programme.title: Celebrity: A 21st Century Story
programme.desc: The documentary charting the transformation of celebrity examines how people capitalised on the digital revolution in order to sidestep the traditional routes to fame. When the old tabloid machinery began to lose power, celebrities made the move to the digital press - and soon after pop acts were making a buzz on the internet. Meanwhile, structured reality shows dominated the younger demographic - and all of these things came together through social media. All of a sudden there was a new way of becoming famous

programme.title: Celebrity: A 21st Century Story
programme.desc: Having learned how to sidestep the conventional media machinery, celebrities acquired the tools take charge of their own narrative on social media. In 2016, actress Meghan Markle became Prince Harry's girlfriend. They publicly confirmed their relationship and swiftly denounced the press. Meanwhile, Donald Trump became US President, taking everything he'd learned from business and media to his role, including catchphrases and shock tactics. Ed Balls showed his moves on the Strictly dancefloor, Rachel Johnson entered the Big Brother house, and her father Stanley the I'm a Celebrity jungle, so while celebrities started playing politics, politicians were seemingly behaving more and more like celebrities

programme.title: Last Woman on Earth with Sara Pascoe
programme.desc: The comedian travels the world to meet people doing rare crafts and jobs that are rapidly becoming lost skills, and tries to pick up their unusual talents. Her journey begins in Cuba, where she finds many professions are being put at risk by increased commercialism and meets a mattress magician, a cigar factory reader and a family who climb giant coconut trees to make a local sweet

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The first of two special Christmas episodes of the culinary challenge, in which eight finalists and semi-finalists from past years return to face a festive test of their skills. The first episode features Danny Parker from the 2014 series, 2017's Louisa Ellis, 2009's Marianne Lumb, and 2018 semi-finalist William Chilila. Monica Galetti sets them the task of making festive canapés, before they move on to the dreaded skills test and a seasonally themed two-course menu

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Aled Jones at 50
programme.desc: As Aled Jones turns 50, this special programme looks back at his life and celebrates his remarkable musical career since making a name for himself as a young soprano. Throughout the programme, Aled discovers archive of himself as a boy he had no idea existed, and also reflects on some of the highlights of his career, singing for Pope John Paul, the Royal family and at the wedding of Bob Geldof and Paula Yates

programme.title: Caribbean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer travels around the islands and mainland coast of the Caribbean Sea, beginning on Hispaniola. He joins the Dominican Republic police's anti-narcotics division, before crossing the border to Haiti to see the notorious tented slums of Cite Soleil, while also discovering a vibrant, colourful and thriving side to the country. He ends this leg of his journey in Puerto Rico, an archipelago that is a territory of the United States, and examines the legacy of bombing and weapons testing on the island of Vieques

programme.title: Joanna Lumley in the Land of the Northern Lights
programme.desc: The actress travels up through Norway to Svalbard, the most northerly permanently inhabited place on Earth, where she experiences temperatures approaching minus 30C. Her aim is to fulfil a lifetime ambition to see the natural phenomenon, the Northern Lights. Along the way she explores the fjords of Lofoten, learns to ride a snowmobile and spends a night in a hotel made of ice

programme.title: Max Richter's Sleep (2019)
programme.desc: Cameras follow acclaimed composer and musician Max Richter and his creative partner, artist and film-maker Yulia Mahr, as they navigate an ambitious performance of his eight-hour opus Sleep at an open-air concert in Los Angeles

programme.title: Upstream
programme.desc: Over three years, cameras follow the course of the river Dee in Scotland to its source in the Cairngorm mountains, the highest of any river in Britain. The film, by writer Robert Macfarlane and director Rob Petit, begins in the lowland floodplains of the Dee, before travelling up, against the flow of the river, into Britain's own Arctic wilderness

programme.title: Aled Jones at 50
programme.desc: As Aled Jones turns 50, this special programme looks back at his life and celebrates his remarkable musical career since making a name for himself as a young soprano. Throughout the programme, Aled discovers archive of himself as a boy he had no idea existed, and also reflects on some of the highlights of his career, singing for Pope John Paul, the Royal family and at the wedding of Bob Geldof and Paula Yates

programme.title: Caribbean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer travels around the islands and mainland coast of the Caribbean Sea, beginning on Hispaniola. He joins the Dominican Republic police's anti-narcotics division, before crossing the border to Haiti to see the notorious tented slums of Cite Soleil, while also discovering a vibrant, colourful and thriving side to the country. He ends this leg of his journey in Puerto Rico, an archipelago that is a territory of the United States, and examines the legacy of bombing and weapons testing on the island of Vieques

programme.title: Joanna Lumley in the Land of the Northern Lights
programme.desc: The actress travels up through Norway to Svalbard, the most northerly permanently inhabited place on Earth, where she experiences temperatures approaching minus 30C. Her aim is to fulfil a lifetime ambition to see the natural phenomenon, the Northern Lights. Along the way she explores the fjords of Lofoten, learns to ride a snowmobile and spends a night in a hotel made of ice

programme.title: Winter Walks
programme.desc: Celebrities explore landscapes in Yorkshire and Cumbria. Selina Scott enjoys a gentle walk around Wharfedale in the Yorkshire Dales. Along the way, she learns stories of extraordinary philanthropy, meets walkers and a fisherman, and ends her journey drinking local ale in a pub famed for ferret racing

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a scene depicting a tree forest perfectly mirrored in the cold stillness of winter

programme.title: Secrets of the Museum
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the world-famous Victoria and Albert Museum in London. Only a small amount of the two million items in the V&A's collection are on display to the public. This series gets a glimpse at the treasure trove kept behind closed doors and follows the experts and conservators as they breathe new life into some of these fragile items, such as Pumpie the 100-year-old toy elephant, who needs urgent TLC, and the Christian Dior gowns that get a new lease of life

programme.title: Ian Hislop's Fake News: A True History
programme.desc: The Private Eye editor explores the history of dishonest news reporting over the past 200 years, investigating how the rivalry between press barons Joseph Pulitzer and William Randolph Hearst may have contributed to an actual war. The programme also looks at the influence of technology, from early trick photography to modern deepfakes, and features the owner of a Washington pizza parlour who has been on the receiving end of one of the modern world's most infamous conspiracy theories

programme.title: Murder 24/7
programme.desc: Documentary following murder investigations by Essex police, from the critical first few days right through to arrest and conviction. It begins with the first of three programmes charting the inquiry into the fatal stabbing of 36-year-old Courtney Valentine-Brown in Southend-on-Sea, Essex, as a team of detectives and specialists work around the clock to catch the killers

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Timeshift explores the contemporary quest for ancient truths that has arisen out of Dan Brown's best-selling novel challenging the credibility of Christian doctrine. The programme assesses the explosive response to the book from believers and atheists. With contributions by author Richard Leigh and art critic Brian Sewell

programme.title: Gerry Rafferty: Right Down the Line
programme.desc: A profile of the singer-songwriter, who died in January 2011 and is best remembered for solo hits including Baker Street and Get It Right Next Time, and Stuck in the Middle with You by Stealers Wheel. The programme traces his life through his often autobiographical songs and features contributions by his daughter Martha, brother Jim and friends and colleagues including Billy Connolly, John Byrne and Joe Egan. Narrated by David Tennant

programme.title: Ian Hislop's Fake News: A True History
programme.desc: The Private Eye editor explores the history of dishonest news reporting over the past 200 years, investigating how the rivalry between press barons Joseph Pulitzer and William Randolph Hearst may have contributed to an actual war. The programme also looks at the influence of technology, from early trick photography to modern deepfakes, and features the owner of a Washington pizza parlour who has been on the receiving end of one of the modern world's most infamous conspiracy theories
