programme.title: Winter Walks
programme.desc: Regional series in which celebrities explore landscapes in Yorkshire and Cumbria. Lemn Sissay walks along ancient drovers' roads in Cumbria's Dentdale, meeting locals, including a farmer who collects vintage tractors and a lone bagpiper, and making a challenging climb to England's highest mainline train station

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a picture of a snow-covered forest, withn veautiful leafless trees and a texture you can almost feel

programme.title: Inside Obama's White House
programme.desc: Four-part series giving an account of Barack Obama's successes and failures, as told by his inner circle and chief adversaries, including the story of how he tried to reshape America. The election of the first African American President marked an era of hope, but what happened instead was economic collapse, minimal cooperation from the Republican opposition, and the rise of a populist anti-big government Tea Party. The first edition focuses on how Obama crashed a private meeting of fellow heads of state to persuade the Chinese to tackle climate change at the Copenhagen summit, as well as his struggle to shut down Guantanamo

programme.title: Tutankhamun in Colour
programme.desc: Egyptologist Elizabeth Frood takes viewers back to 1922 to experience the discovery of Tutankhamun's tomb as it happened, with the help of colourised photographs and film footage. The documentary follows the story from the first encounter between Howard Carter and Lord Carnarvon, the discovery of a vast chamber filled with ancient relics to the opening of the burial chamber itself, and reveals how the discoveries made changed how the boy king and Egypt as a nation were viewed around the world

programme.title: Murder 24/7
programme.desc: A man is arrested for the murder of Courtney Valentine-Brown, but during his interview, he names another as being the killer. This sparks a manhunt to locate him, but the search is against the clock, as detectives must find the second suspect before it becomes necessary to either charge or release the first

programme.title: Light and Dark
programme.desc: The first of a two-part documentary in which Professor Jim Al-Khalili examines how some of history's greatest minds began to uncover the secrets of the universe by using and manipulating light. In the first edition, the presenter considers the significance of Greek mathematician Euclid's landmark discovery that light moves in straight lines. He then moves on to Renaissance Italy, where Galileo Galilei unlocked the potential of telescopes in locating Earth's true place within the cosmos, before assessing the implications of Danish astronomer Ole Roemer's groundbreaking finding - that light moves at a finite speed

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: With Gilou facing the possibility of prison, Laure's team try to recover their reputation by taking on a case that no-one else wants - the murder of an identified minor in a run-down part of town. The return of the French detective drama, starring Caroline Proust. In French

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Gilou makes headway as an informer and gets close to Cisco, a dangerous convict coming to the end of his sentence who is being monitored by the police. Josephine Karlsson meets Souleymane, the 13-year-old Moroccan boy accused of the murder Laure and Ali are investigating. In French

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: A look at the life of the 17th-century military and political leader, following him as he challenges Charles I in the English Civil War, and ultimately orders the king's execution. Plus, the programme meets a bunch of oddly named Puritan soldiers, and the great writers Shakespeare and Milton square up for a word battle

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: The life and times of Queen Victoria, following her as she ascends the throne at 18, marries her true love Albert, and has to put up with the open sewers at Buckingham Palace. The programme also catches up with Australian outlaw Ned Kelly, and discovers plans for the Great Hedge of India. Starring Sarah Hadland

programme.title: Operation Ouch!

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: The doctors find out how far the heart can pump blood, while Dr Xand meets a life-saving dog, and Dr Chris solves more medical mysteries in the Ouch-Mobile. Plus, a patient who has a swollen face due to a dental abscess and another who has sliced her finger on an electric fire visit Accident & Emergency

programme.title: The Real Jamie Johnson
programme.desc: The cast of Jamie Johnson travel the UK in search of the country's most talented, inspiring and dedicated young footballers. Lenna Gunning Williams meets up with Janaye, a talented player who is training hard at a top independent academy. Guided by her coaches, Mollie and Rosie, Janaye is trialling over the summer for some top women's clubs - but will she make the cut?

programme.title: Deadly Dinosaurs with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve is on a mission to unearth the dinosaurs that were far deadlier than they first appeared. He discovers how the Sinornithosaurus hides a deadly dental secret, races a Velociraptor in a climbing challenge and puts Argentinasaurus to the ultimate test

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including West Bromwich Albion v Arsenal and Tottenham Hotspur v Leeds United. The north London rivals involved in these fixtures have enjoyed contrasting fortunes this season, with Spurs near the top of the table, and Arsenal floundering in the lower reaches following a series of home defeats

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Sunday Worship
programme.desc: The Very Rev Henry Hull leads the ecumenical service for the Second Sunday of Christmas from Down Cathedral in Downpatrick, Co Down. With hymns from the Songs of Praise archives from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits a large two-bedroom apartment in Battersea, south-west London, while Martin Roberts looks over plans to develop a house in Winsford, Cheshire

programme.title: Planes: Fire & Rescue (2014)
programme.desc: A racing plane suffers damage to his engine, and realises he can never compete again. He starts a new career as a member of a firefighting squadron, which proves even more dangerous than he expected when the aircraft have to deal with a massive forest blaze. Animated adventure sequel, with the voices of Dane Cook and Ed Harris

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross oversees oversees proceedings at the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells as two teams scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell on for profit. Experts Philip Serrell and Thomas Plant attempt to guide the sides to success at auction in Cardiff

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins explores St Paul's Cathedral, with music including hymns sung by the Cathedral Choir and performances by Aled Jones and the Priests, and Daniel O'Donnell

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood's alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture continues with the letter M. Various BBC gardening presenters are mesmerised by magnolias, marvel at the monkey puzzle tree and meet a marigold enthusiast

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton heads to the Welsh Borders to help a couple with a budget of £775,000 find a countryside home to raise their young son and establish a glamping retreat business

programme.title: Pete's Dragon (2016)
programme.desc: Ranger Grace finds an orphaned 10-year-old boy in the forest, where he has been ever since the death of his parents five years earlier. What's more, he tells her he has been living with a giant, fire-breathing dragon. Could her father's tales of a mythical beast be true after all? Fantasy adventure, starring Bryce Dallas Howard, Robert Redford and Oakes Fegley

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway goes on a walk from his native Barry along the coast road to Rhoose Point. Later, he heads north for a trek among the foothills of Snowdonia's Carneddau mountains, along the Huw Tom Trail

programme.title: Aled Jones at 50
programme.desc: As Aled Jones turns 50, this special programme looks back at his life and celebrates his musical career since making a name for himself as a young soprano. Throughout the programme, Aled discovers archive of himself as a boy he had no idea existed, and also reflects on some of the highlights of his career, singing for Pope John Paul, the Royal family and at the wedding of Bob Geldof and Paula Yates

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher looks back at 2020, a difficult year when the countryside became a lifeline for unprecedented numbers during the pandemic, while wildlife made their homes in curious places during lockdown. Sean also catches up with young people at the forefront of the battle to protect the environment

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The programme returns with Fiona Bruce and the team visiting Forty Hall in Enfield, north London, where items brought in for assessment include a set of Star Wars toys, and a policeman's helmet worn by John Lennon. There is also a rock crystal Faberge cigarette case complete with a ruby, an early post-box, and a poster for one of Pink Floyd's very first concerts

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a documentary revealing how the forces of nature drive, shape and support the Earth's great diversity of life. The first edition examines volcanoes, which responsible for both for the planet's breathable atmosphere and the oceans, but are also the architects of the planet, with over 80% of the Earth's surface being the result of magma bursting up from the molten interior - providing a platform for life

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: Marie-Andree Leclerc falls under the spell of Charles Sobhraj while on holiday in India and is persuaded to move to Bangkok to live with him, but he does not give her the life he promised he. When Charles does takes Marie-Andrée to the beach, he enlists her to befriend an Australian couple, who he drugs and robs of their money and passports. Drama, starring Tahar Rahim and Jenna Coleman

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the latest Premier League action, featuring Chelsea v Manchester City, Burnley v Fulham and Newcastle United v Leicester City. Neither Chelsea nor Manchester City have found the consistency yet this season to suggest that winning the title is within their grasp, but victory against their rivals here would send out a statement to the rest of the challengers

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: The second of two festive specials of the comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll. Winnie has not been sleeping well, and wants to tell Agnes about her dreams, so when her house is later burgled and her mother's wedding ring stolen, Buster and Dermot plan to take matters into their own hands to expose the thief. Cathy has a significant birthday coming up - and is far from happy about it

programme.title: Escape from Alcatraz (1979)
programme.desc: A cunning convict with a history of making escape attempts is transferred to the notorious high-security island prison off the California coast, from which no one has ever broken out. Continually at odds with the brutal warden, he and three fellow prisoners hatch a plan to make it to freedom on an improvised raft. Thriller, based on a true story, starring Clint Eastwood, Patrick McGoohan, Roberts Blossom and Fred Ward

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Dan Walker and Louise Minchin

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville return to examine more disastrous holidays and travel stories, beginning by hearing from passengers whose dream cruise was ruined by a fire on board - which meant missing out the highlight of the trip

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the progress of a semi-detached in Farnworth near Bolton, a one-bedroom apartment in Trent Vale, Stoke-on-Trent, and a listed building in Maidstone, Kent

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: After leaving her native South Africa when her young daughter became critically ill, Zelease Ballentine Brain felt her young children needed to live in a place where being outside in the sunshine was the norm. Husband Matt was naturally cautious, and needed to see that Australia would give the family all that Zelease had talked about. Could a trial week swing him round to her way of thinking and, three years on, where have they decided to settle? Presented by Nicki Chapman

programme.title: Moment of Proof
programme.desc: The stories of pivotal moments in police investigations when detectives realise they have cracked a case, told through interviews and dramatic reconstructions. In the first edition, a single text message - hidden among thousands - nails a serial fraudster, and an extraordinary surveillance operation leads straight to the kidnappers and their hostage

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Richard Madley and Stephanie Connell help the teams look for items at the Brackley Antiques Cellar in Northamptonshire, while Eric Knowles learns about the art of signwriting

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Lady Felicia and Mrs McCarthy are taken hostage when a midnight train robbery goes awry. Crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Sorcha Cusack and Nancy Carroll

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers share nostalgic dishes that conjure up memories of childhood, holidays and bygone days, preparing a Lancashire hotpot just like Dave's mother used to make

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A health-conscious couple from London enlist Sonali Shah to help them spend £1.3million on a detached property in Surrey with enough land to create a wellness retreat

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: The return of the programme celebrating inspirational farming families and the rural events where they showcase their hard work. Sheep and arable farmers John and Ed head to Moseley Farmers' Market, but when they arrive their pitch location threatens to put a dent in their earnings. The Styan family, meanwhile, need to sell as much veg as possible for cash to make some much-needed repairs to their windblown farm

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers returns with the programme in which five dealers bid against one another to buy intriguing items brought in by members of the public. The opening edition features a tribal mask, avintage seed drill, adecorative press, a toy horse and a mysterious Edwardian piece

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas host the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville creates a special blend of snuff, but will his customers notice any beneficial effects? The ladies get dressed up for line-dancing, while Gastric is prompted to go on a diet. A furtive Mr Newbold thinks he's found a pastime to share with Mrs Rossi - away from the prying eyes of Mrs Featherstone. However, a determined Black Widow enlists assistance from Granville and Leroy for a crash-course in how to ride a bicycle. David Jason, Stephanie Cole, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Maggie Ollerenshaw star

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A desperate Stacey turns on Ruby as her concern for Kush worsens. Martin defends his wife, and when Ruby tries ringing, things are made even worse when Arthur answers. Callum's luck has run out as Jack delivers bad news and the cracks start to show as Sonia struggles to cope at

programme.title: Nightshifters
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the hidden world of night-time workers - those unsung heroes who occupy the graveyard shift in a wide range of jobs. The first programme features whelk fishermen leaving Saundersfoot harbour, train cleaners in Cardiff and a lone Swansea taxi driver employed over the witching hour. This invisible army works in the dark to make sure the day runs like clockwork

programme.title: Traces
programme.desc: Emma Hedges' return to Dundee to start a new job brings her mother's unsolved murder into sharp focus as she fights to bring the killer to justice. However, before she makes the journey back to her home town, a fatal fire in a nightclub proves ominous. Crime drama by Amelia Bullmore, based on an original idea by Val McDermid, starring Molly Windsor, Laura Fraser, Martin Compston and Jennifer Spence

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: While appearing on a chat show discussing a year in lockdown, Michael and David are pained to discover someone they respect is not a fan of their work. David's intention to go to work and Michael's plans to visit a friend abroad are both scuppered by last-minute travel rule changes. The return of the comedy, starring David Tennant and Michael Sheen

programme.title: Accused: A Mother on Trial
programme.desc: Documentary following the case of Alaskan woman Ashley Ard as she faces the prospect of up to 99 years in prison for murdering her newborn child. Her lawyer Rex Lamont-Butler compiles the defence with the help of local private detective Tank Jones, who revisits the park where the baby was abandoned, convinced that Ashley is not who the public think she is

programme.title: The Science(ish) of Stranger Things
programme.desc: Comedian Sophie Duker, presenter Rick Edwards and physicist Michael Brooks dissect the science behind the hit Netflix sci-fi series, sorting the nanoscience from the nonsense

programme.title: The Wall Versus Celebrities
programme.desc: Danny Dyer hosts this second special celebrity edition of the game show, which sees Last Leg presenter Alex Brooker and actress Sally Lindsay teaming up to win as much cash as possible for their chosen charities. But their chances rest on the unpredictability of the Wall and their unflinching trust in one another

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Dan Walker and Louise Minchin

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: Stories of holiday-makers left stranded at the airport because their Covid-19 test results came back too late, and people who arrived at a hotel only to discover it was closed

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit two neighbouring cottages in Derbyshire, a terrace in Bolton and a semi in Leeds before they are sold under the hammer

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Claire Duffy wanted the best in life for her young family, especially youngest daughter Abbie, who had recently received a cystic fibrosis diagnosis. She was sure that the warm weather and outdoor lifestyle of New Zealand would do wonders for Abbie's health, but had to convince home bird husband Russell that moving halfway across the world was a viable option. Two years later, have they made the leap, or was the pull of home just too strong? Nicki Chapman catches up with the family to find out

programme.title: Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A pair of pyjamas reveal the shocking true identity of an attacker who left his victim for dead, and a bright yellow baseball cap puts a daylight robber behind bars

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Caroline Hawley and Roo Irvine help two teams look for items in an antiques centre in the Lancashire town of Eccleston. Eric Knowles overseeing proceedings and also attempts a spot of Morris Dancing, discovering the history of the humble clog and the part it played in Lancashire's industrial heritage

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A new musical director for the bell-ringers of St Mary's leads to discord, disharmony and the blood-curdling sound of murder. Mystery, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers cook comfort food dishes straight from the oven, including a new take on steak and chips and sticky gingerbread

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a house hunter from Surrey to find a new home in Pembrokeshire, and finds her a remote period house with spectacular views within her budget of £900,000

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Hexham's fortnightly market brings hope of selling some lamb and beef to farmer Andrew and his daughter Emma, and Lee and Shelly are selling produce from their Tamworth pigs

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents the programme in which five dealers bid against one another to buy a pedal car, an Edwardian card table, a quirky voice box and a mystery pot

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Jack sets his plan in motion and manages to negotiate an advantageous deal, meaning he soon has good news for Denise. Stacey is in for a surprise, Whitney has a choice to make and Ruby has to come up with an explanation for Martin. Ash has advice for Sonia after she ends another exhausting shift at the hospital

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Sacha buries his head in the sand when Beka is taken ill on New Year's Eve. Kian is on edge as Sarah Jane avoids his calls, and his stash is running out. He tries to convince Jac he is fine when she worries that their relationship is the problem. It is total chaos on AAU, and the F1s are daunted when Hanssen leaves them alone to face their first New Year's night shift

programme.title: Carol Vorderman: Closer to Home
programme.desc: Carol Vorderman takes a road trip across her home patch of North Wales and reflects on how the region has shaped her. The journey begins on the coast in her hometown of Prestatyn, sharing memories of her mother's life as a divorcee raising three children alone in the 1960s and reflecting on how the lives of women have changed since then

programme.title: Traces
programme.desc: As Kathy and Sarah work on their reports for the Secrets nightclub fire, the emerging picture is that two of the deaths were accidental, but the third victim was dead before the fire started. Sarah's report has some damning things to say about the safety of the building, but soon feeling overwhelmed by the police interest she wonders what she's started. Emma is cautioned by DI McKinven to stop sleuthing, as the stars align to put Daniel Macafee in her path - for whom she feels a deep connection. It later transpires that Daniel harbours a guilty conscience as he was employed by the company that refurbished Secrets. Crime drama by Amelia Bullmore, starring Molly Windsor, Laura Fraser and Jennifer Spence

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: After discovering the network's plan and learning they will not be going to America, David and Michael are dismayed to learn who will be going in their place. The guys are also upset to find out how well Simon is doing without them

programme.title: Accused: A Mother on Trial
programme.desc: Rex searches for evidence to implicate Kennard in the crime. Details of Ashley and Kennard's past reveal why she might be covering up for him. Kennard finally breaks his silence

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: Contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing a cash prize. Helping them this time round are Stacey Dooley, Roman Kemp, Omid Djalili, Judy Murray, Owain Wyn Evans, Kate Humble and Vikkstar

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including West Bromwich Albion v Arsenal and Tottenham Hotspur v Leeds United. The north London rivals involved in these fixtures have enjoyed contrasting fortunes this season, with Spurs near the top of the table, and Arsenal floundering in the lower reaches following a series of home defeats

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Sunday Worship
programme.desc: The Very Rev Henry Hull leads the ecumenical service for the Second Sunday of Christmas from Down Cathedral in Downpatrick, Co Down. With hymns from the Songs of Praise archives from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Reflections at the Quay
programme.desc: The Rev Jane Howitt of St Rollox and Fr Dermot Preston of St Aloysius, both Glasgow, present from the foyer of BBC Glasgow, with live music by Rachel Hair and Ron Jappy.

programme.title: Planes: Fire & Rescue (2014)
programme.desc: A racing plane suffers damage to his engine, and realises he can never compete again. He starts a new career as a member of a firefighting squadron, which proves even more dangerous than he expected when the aircraft have to deal with a massive forest blaze. Animated adventure sequel, with the voices of Dane Cook and Ed Harris

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross oversees oversees proceedings at the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells as two teams scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell on for profit. Experts Philip Serrell and Thomas Plant attempt to guide the sides to success at auction in Cardiff

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins explores St Paul's Cathedral, with music including hymns sung by the Cathedral Choir and performances by Aled Jones and the Priests, and Daniel O'Donnell

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton heads to the Welsh Borders to help a couple with a budget of £775,000 find a countryside home to raise their young son and establish a glamping retreat business

programme.title: Pete's Dragon (2016)
programme.desc: Ranger Grace finds an orphaned 10-year-old boy in the forest, where he has been ever since the death of his parents five years earlier. What's more, he tells her he has been living with a giant, fire-breathing dragon. Could her father's tales of a mythical beast be true after all? Fantasy adventure, starring Bryce Dallas Howard, Robert Redford and Oakes Fegley

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The team are in Leamington Spa to help Robert and Gemma and their daughter Darcey, who is afraid of the family's nettle-strewn, overgrown garden

programme.title: Penguins: Meet the Family
programme.desc: Documentary following penguins living in unexpected environments, including satellite imagery of previously undiscovered colonies of flightless birds. Among their habitats are dense forests, desert burrows and city streets. The programme reveals the challenges they face in making their homes in these challenging environments, from evading predators to scaling rocks, and captures the lifelong bonds formed between family groups

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher looks back at 2020, a difficult year when the countryside became a lifeline for unprecedented numbers during the pandemic, while wildlife made their homes in curious places during lockdown. Sean also catches up with young people at the forefront of the battle to protect the environment

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The programme returns with Fiona Bruce and the team visiting Forty Hall in Enfield, north London, where items brought in for assessment include a set of Star Wars toys, and a policeman's helmet worn by John Lennon. There is also a rock crystal Faberge cigarette case complete with a ruby, an early post-box, and a poster for one of Pink Floyd's very first concerts

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a documentary revealing how the forces of nature drive, shape and support the Earth's great diversity of life. The first edition examines volcanoes, which responsible for both for the planet's breathable atmosphere and the oceans, but are also the architects of the planet, with over 80% of the Earth's surface being the result of magma bursting up from the molten interior - providing a platform for life

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: Marie-Andree Leclerc falls under the spell of Charles Sobhraj while on holiday in India and is persuaded to move to Bangkok to live with him, but he does not give her the life he promised he. When Charles does takes Marie-Andrée to the beach, he enlists her to befriend an Australian couple, who he drugs and robs of their money and passports. Drama, starring Tahar Rahim and Jenna Coleman

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the latest Premier League action, featuring Chelsea v Manchester City, Burnley v Fulham and Newcastle United v Leicester City. Neither Chelsea nor Manchester City have found the consistency yet this season to suggest that winning the title is within their grasp, but victory against their rivals here would send out a statement to the rest of the challengers

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: The second of two festive specials of the comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll. Winnie has not been sleeping well, and wants to tell Agnes about her dreams, so when her house is later burgled and her mother's wedding ring stolen, Buster and Dermot plan to take matters into their own hands to expose the thief. Cathy has a significant birthday coming up - and is far from happy about it

programme.title: Escape from Alcatraz (1979)
programme.desc: A cunning convict with a history of making escape attempts is transferred to the notorious high-security island prison off the California coast, from which no one has ever broken out. Continually at odds with the brutal warden, he and three fellow prisoners hatch a plan to make it to freedom on an improvised raft. Thriller, based on a true story, starring Clint Eastwood, Patrick McGoohan, Roberts Blossom and Fred Ward

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Dan Walker and Louise Minchin

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville return to examine more disastrous holidays and travel stories, beginning by hearing from passengers whose dream cruise was ruined by a fire on board - which meant missing out the highlight of the trip

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the progress of a semi-detached in Farnworth near Bolton, a one-bedroom apartment in Trent Vale, Stoke-on-Trent, and a listed building in Maidstone, Kent

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: After leaving her native South Africa when her young daughter became critically ill, Zelease Ballentine Brain felt her young children needed to live in a place where being outside in the sunshine was the norm. Husband Matt was naturally cautious, and needed to see that Australia would give the family all that Zelease had talked about. Could a trial week swing him round to her way of thinking and, three years on, where have they decided to settle? Presented by Nicki Chapman

programme.title: Moment of Proof
programme.desc: The stories of pivotal moments in police investigations when detectives realise they have cracked a case, told through interviews and dramatic reconstructions. In the first edition, a single text message - hidden among thousands - nails a serial fraudster, and an extraordinary surveillance operation leads straight to the kidnappers and their hostage

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Richard Madley and Stephanie Connell help the teams look for items at the Brackley Antiques Cellar in Northamptonshire, while Eric Knowles learns about the art of signwriting

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Lady Felicia and Mrs McCarthy are taken hostage when a midnight train robbery goes awry. Crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Sorcha Cusack and Nancy Carroll

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers share nostalgic dishes that conjure up memories of childhood, holidays and bygone days, preparing a Lancashire hotpot just like Dave's mother used to make

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A health-conscious couple from London enlist Sonali Shah to help them spend £1.3million on a detached property in Surrey with enough land to create a wellness retreat

programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a documentary revealing how the forces of nature drive, shape and support the Earth's great diversity of life. The first edition examines volcanoes, which responsible for both for the planet's breathable atmosphere and the oceans, but are also the architects of the planet, with over 80% of the Earth's surface being the result of magma bursting up from the molten interior - providing a platform for life

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: Marie-Andree Leclerc falls under the spell of Charles Sobhraj while on holiday in India and is persuaded to move to Bangkok to live with him, but he does not give her the life he promised he. When Charles does takes Marie-Andrée to the beach, he enlists her to befriend an Australian couple, who he drugs and robs of their money and passports. Drama, starring Tahar Rahim and Jenna Coleman

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the latest Premier League action, featuring Chelsea v Manchester City, Burnley v Fulham and Newcastle United v Leicester City. Neither Chelsea nor Manchester City have found the consistency yet this season to suggest that winning the title is within their grasp, but victory against their rivals here would send out a statement to the rest of the challengers

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: The second of two festive specials of the comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll. Winnie has not been sleeping well, and wants to tell Agnes about her dreams, so when her house is later burgled and her mother's wedding ring stolen, Buster and Dermot plan to take matters into their own hands to expose the thief. Cathy has a significant birthday coming up - and is far from happy about it

programme.title: Escape from Alcatraz (1979)
programme.desc: A cunning convict with a history of making escape attempts is transferred to the notorious high-security island prison off the California coast, from which no one has ever broken out. Continually at odds with the brutal warden, he and three fellow prisoners hatch a plan to make it to freedom on an improvised raft. Thriller, based on a true story, starring Clint Eastwood, Patrick McGoohan, Roberts Blossom and Fred Ward

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Dan Walker and Louise Minchin

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville return to examine more disastrous holidays and travel stories, beginning by hearing from passengers whose dream cruise was ruined by a fire on board - which meant missing out the highlight of the trip

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the progress of a semi-detached in Farnworth near Bolton, a one-bedroom apartment in Trent Vale, Stoke-on-Trent, and a listed building in Maidstone, Kent

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: After leaving her native South Africa when her young daughter became critically ill, Zelease Ballentine Brain felt her young children needed to live in a place where being outside in the sunshine was the norm. Husband Matt was naturally cautious, and needed to see that Australia would give the family all that Zelease had talked about. Could a trial week swing him round to her way of thinking and, three years on, where have they decided to settle? Presented by Nicki Chapman

programme.title: Moment of Proof
programme.desc: The stories of pivotal moments in police investigations when detectives realise they have cracked a case, told through interviews and dramatic reconstructions. In the first edition, a single text message - hidden among thousands - nails a serial fraudster, and an extraordinary surveillance operation leads straight to the kidnappers and their hostage

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Richard Madley and Stephanie Connell help the teams look for items at the Brackley Antiques Cellar in Northamptonshire, while Eric Knowles learns about the art of signwriting

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Lady Felicia and Mrs McCarthy are taken hostage when a midnight train robbery goes awry. Crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Sorcha Cusack and Nancy Carroll

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers share nostalgic dishes that conjure up memories of childhood, holidays and bygone days, preparing a Lancashire hotpot just like Dave's mother used to make

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A health-conscious couple from London enlist Sonali Shah to help them spend £1.3million on a detached property in Surrey with enough land to create a wellness retreat

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: The return of the programme celebrating inspirational farming families and the rural events where they showcase their hard work. Sheep and arable farmers John and Ed head to Moseley Farmers' Market, but when they arrive their pitch location threatens to put a dent in their earnings. The Styan family, meanwhile, need to sell as much veg as possible for cash to make some much-needed repairs to their windblown farm

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers returns with the programme in which five dealers bid against one another to buy intriguing items brought in by members of the public. The opening edition features a tribal mask, avintage seed drill, adecorative press, a toy horse and a mysterious Edwardian piece

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas host the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Chronicles of the Glens
programme.desc: Colin Morgan narrates a documentary charting a year in the Glens of Antrim, following the changing landscape, wildlife and people. The first episode focuses on autumn, where farmers in Fairhead are preparing calves for market, while residents group In the coastal village of Cushendall are preparing for a Halloween fundraising event at Tiveragh allotment

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A desperate Stacey turns on Ruby as her concern for Kush worsens. Martin defends his wife, and when Ruby tries ringing, things are made even worse when Arthur answers. Callum's luck has run out as Jack delivers bad news and the cracks start to show as Sonia struggles to cope at

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville creates a special blend of snuff, but will his customers notice any beneficial effects? The ladies get dressed up for line-dancing, while Gastric is prompted to go on a diet. A furtive Mr Newbold thinks he's found a pastime to share with Mrs Rossi - away from the prying eyes of Mrs Featherstone. However, a determined Black Widow enlists assistance from Granville and Leroy for a crash-course in how to ride a bicycle. David Jason, Stephanie Cole, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Maggie Ollerenshaw star

programme.title: Traces
programme.desc: Emma Hedges' return to Dundee to start a new job brings her mother's unsolved murder into sharp focus as she fights to bring the killer to justice. However, before she makes the journey back to her home town, a fatal fire in a nightclub proves ominous. Crime drama by Amelia Bullmore, based on an original idea by Val McDermid, starring Molly Windsor, Laura Fraser, Martin Compston and Jennifer Spence

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: While appearing on a chat show discussing a year in lockdown, Michael and David are pained to discover someone they respect is not a fan of their work. David's intention to go to work and Michael's plans to visit a friend abroad are both scuppered by last-minute travel rule changes. The return of the comedy, starring David Tennant and Michael Sheen

programme.title: Tricked-Out Tractors
programme.desc: Tylor Cartin, Stephen Lennon and Darren Bailey customise tractors, tackling mechanical problems and performing major makeovers. Irish tractor-pulling champ Darren Bailey brings in his monster machine to get it into shape, needing it to be bigger and better to keep his crown

programme.title: Accused: A Mother on Trial
programme.desc: Documentary following the case of Alaskan woman Ashley Ard as she faces the prospect of up to 99 years in prison for murdering her newborn child. Her lawyer Rex Lamont-Butler compiles the defence with the help of local private detective Tank Jones, who revisits the park where the baby was abandoned, convinced that Ashley is not who the public think she is

programme.title: The Science(ish) of Stranger Things
programme.desc: Comedian Sophie Duker, presenter Rick Edwards and physicist Michael Brooks dissect the science behind the hit Netflix sci-fi series, sorting the nanoscience from the nonsense

programme.title: The Wall Versus Celebrities
programme.desc: Danny Dyer hosts this second special celebrity edition of the game show, which sees Last Leg presenter Alex Brooker and actress Sally Lindsay teaming up to win as much cash as possible for their chosen charities. But their chances rest on the unpredictability of the Wall and their unflinching trust in one another

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Dan Walker and Louise Minchin

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: Stories of holiday-makers left stranded at the airport because their Covid-19 test results came back too late, and people who arrived at a hotel only to discover it was closed

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit two neighbouring cottages in Derbyshire, a terrace in Bolton and a semi in Leeds before they are sold under the hammer

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Claire Duffy wanted the best in life for her young family, especially youngest daughter Abbie, who had recently received a cystic fibrosis diagnosis. She was sure that the warm weather and outdoor lifestyle of New Zealand would do wonders for Abbie's health, but had to convince home bird husband Russell that moving halfway across the world was a viable option. Two years later, have they made the leap, or was the pull of home just too strong? Nicki Chapman catches up with the family to find out

programme.title: Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A pair of pyjamas reveal the shocking true identity of an attacker who left his victim for dead, and a bright yellow baseball cap puts a daylight robber behind bars

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Caroline Hawley and Roo Irvine help two teams look for items in an antiques centre in the Lancashire town of Eccleston. Eric Knowles overseeing proceedings and also attempts a spot of Morris Dancing, discovering the history of the humble clog and the part it played in Lancashire's industrial heritage

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A new musical director for the bell-ringers of St Mary's leads to discord, disharmony and the blood-curdling sound of murder. Mystery, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers cook comfort food dishes straight from the oven, including a new take on steak and chips and sticky gingerbread

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a house hunter from Surrey to find a new home in Pembrokeshire, and finds her a remote period house with spectacular views within her budget of £900,000

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Hexham's fortnightly market brings hope of selling some lamb and beef to farmer Andrew and his daughter Emma, and Lee and Shelly are selling produce from their Tamworth pigs

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents the programme in which five dealers bid against one another to buy a pedal car, an Edwardian card table, a quirky voice box and a mystery pot

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Jack sets his plan in motion and manages to negotiate an advantageous deal, meaning he soon has good news for Denise. Stacey is in for a surprise, Whitney has a choice to make and Ruby has to come up with an explanation for Martin. Ash has advice for Sonia after she ends another exhausting shift at the hospital

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Sacha buries his head in the sand when Beka is taken ill on New Year's Eve. Kian is on edge as Sarah Jane avoids his calls, and his stash is running out. He tries to convince Jac he is fine when she worries that their relationship is the problem. It is total chaos on AAU, and the F1s are daunted when Hanssen leaves them alone to face their first New Year's night shift

programme.title: The Crash Detectives
programme.desc: Behind the scenes with Gwent Police's dedicated forensic collision investigators as they deal with serious incidents on the roads. In this edition, a hit-and-run leaves a cyclist fighting for her life, while mystery surrounds the death of a delivery driver on a busy dual carriageway

programme.title: Traces
programme.desc: As Kathy and Sarah work on their reports for the Secrets nightclub fire, the emerging picture is that two of the deaths were accidental, but the third victim was dead before the fire started. Sarah's report has some damning things to say about the safety of the building, but soon feeling overwhelmed by the police interest she wonders what she's started. Emma is cautioned by DI McKinven to stop sleuthing, as the stars align to put Daniel Macafee in her path - for whom she feels a deep connection. It later transpires that Daniel harbours a guilty conscience as he was employed by the company that refurbished Secrets. Crime drama by Amelia Bullmore, starring Molly Windsor, Laura Fraser and Jennifer Spence

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: After discovering the network's plan and learning they will not be going to America, David and Michael are dismayed to learn who will be going in their place. The guys are also upset to find out how well Simon is doing without them

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: Stephen Walker meets some of Northern Ireland's oldest citizens as he talks to four women and one man born in 1921, the same year that the state came into existence. They share experiences of serving as a tail gunner on a Lancaster bomber or a nurse in the Blitz during the Second World War, as well as tales of romance in the dancehalls of the 1950s

programme.title: Accused: A Mother on Trial
programme.desc: Rex searches for evidence to implicate Kennard in the crime. Details of Ashley and Kennard's past reveal why she might be covering up for him. Kennard finally breaks his silence

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: Contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing a cash prize. Helping them this time round are Stacey Dooley, Roman Kemp, Omid Djalili, Judy Murray, Owain Wyn Evans, Kate Humble and Vikkstar

programme.title: Lords Questions
programme.desc: Coverage of the last question session of 2020 in the House of Lords

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Former Governor of the Bank of England Mark Carney charts how financial value has come to be held in higher esteem than human value, beginning by assessing what value actually means. How looks at whether the way it is assessed both shapes human values and constrains choices, and looks at how the markets affect society. Introduced by Anita Anand

programme.desc: The Digital, Culture, Media and Sport select committee session on the economics of music streaming with evidence from Colin Young, Tom Gray, Tom Frederikse, Guy Garvey, Ed O'Brien, Nadine Shah, from Tuesday 24 November.

programme.desc: Stephen Sackur speaks to Lt General HR McMaster who served as Mr Trump's national security adviser until he was fired in 2018. Will the Trump years be seen as an aberration or a marker of change?

programme.title: Washington Journal
programme.desc: Highlights of the week from the USA

programme.title: Westminster Hall
programme.desc: Coverage of a debate on the future of football in Westminster Hall, from Wednesday 25 November.

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: The Science and Technology select committee session on UK Science, Research and Technology Capability and Influence in Global Disease Outbreaks focusing on the new strain of Covid-19 in the UK with evidence from Professor Peter Horby, Chair of the New and Emerging Respiratory Virus Threats Advisory Group (NERVTAG), Professor Wendy Barclay, Head of the Department of Infectious Disease, Imperial College London and Professor Neil Ferguson OBE, Director at MRC Centre for Global Infectious Disease Analysis, Imperial College London, from Wednesday 23 December.

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by Alicia McCarthy

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: Dr Chris Whitty, the Chief Medical Officer for England, delivering a lecture at Gresham College on 'the Geography of Ill Health', from Wednesday 25 November

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Joint session of the Health and Social Care and Science and Technology committees. MPs will hear from Prof Devi Sridhar, Professor of Global Public Health at the University of Edinburgh, and Health and Social Care Secretary Matt Hancock, from Tuesday 24 November.

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: In his second Reith Lecture, Mark Carney, the former Governor of the Bank of England, takes his audience back to the high drama of the financial crisis of 2008, which ended a period when bankers saw themselves as unassailable Masters of the Universe. More than a decade on, how much have the bankers changed their ways? How far has the financial sector changed? Dr Carney says that society must remain vigilant and resist the 'three lies of finance'. If not, it will live with a system that is ill-prepared for the next crisis. Presented by Anita Anand

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Scientists have discovered water on the sunlit surface of the moon for the first time but does it matter? The moon is back in vogue in terms of space exploration - the US says it will put astronauts back on the lunar surface by 2024. It's supposed to be the precursor to a manned mission to Mars. Stephen Sackur speaks to Jacob Bleacher, Chief Exploration Scientist at NASA. In this time of pandemic and climate change here on earth is space exploration a potential lifeline or a massive vanity project?

programme.title: Welsh First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Recorded coverage from the Welsh Assembly as First Minister Mark Drakeford tackles queries on issues affecting life in Wales

programme.title: House of Lords
programme.desc: The statement in the House of Lords on the Integrated Review of Security, Defence, Development and Foreign Policy, from earlier today

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Debate from the chamber

programme.title: America This Week
programme.desc: C-SPAN's coverage of the Senate Judiciary committee hearing on the role of tech giants during the presidential election campaign with Facebook CEO Mark Zuckerberg and Twitter CEO Jack Dorsey, from Tuesday 17 November.

programme.title: Westminster Hall
programme.desc: Westminster Hall - Funding for Transport for London Coverage of a debate in Westminster Hall on funding for Transport for London, from Monday 23 November

programme.title: Westminster Hall
programme.desc: Westminster Hall - Funding for Transport for London Coverage of a debate in Westminster Hall on funding for Transport for London, from Monday 23 November

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: In a special edition of the programme, HARDtalk is in northwest England, to the area known as the Lake District. The landscape is beautiful but is not wild. The fields have been shaped by generations of shepherds and stockmen. Stephen Sackur speaks to James Rebanks whose farm has been in his family's hands for at least 600 years. He has become a nationally renowned advocate for a better kind of farming that is more sustainable and environmentally responsible. But are his ideas compatible with putting affordable food on all of our tables?

programme.title: Northern Ireland Assembly
programme.desc: Coverage of proceedings at the Northern Ireland Assembly at Stormont

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Mark Carney, the former Governor of the Bank of England, observes that the pandemic has forced states to confront how people value health, wealth and opportunity. During the first few months of the crisis, most states chose to value human life more than the economic well-being of the nation-state. But if that seems to be changing how do we assess value in this sense? Dr Carney elucidates surprising differences in the financial value put on a human life in different nations - and goes on to argue that this reductionist approach fails to take into account deeper thinking about the worth of human existence. Presented by Anita Anand

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: The short debate in the House of Commons marking the 60th anniversary of Coronation Street, opened by the MP and former Coronation Street actor, Tracy Brabin, from Wednesday 2 December

programme.desc: Conservative-led debate in the Welsh Parliament on the impact of Covid on health services, from Wednesday 25 November. First shown on BBC Parliament on Saturday 28 November

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Coverage of the backbench business debate in the House of Commons on the regulation and prevention of online harms, from Thursday 19 November.

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: As soon as he emerged from his coma Alexei Navalny, the Russian opposition leader apparently poisoned by novichok nerve agent, expressed his determination to return to Moscow. But what future is there for an anti-Putin political movement in a country where dissent is all too often seriously bad for your health? Stephen Sackur speaks to Leonid Volkov, opposition politician and chief of staff to Mr Navalny. Is there any weakening of the Kremlin's grip on power?

programme.title: House of Lords
programme.desc: Peers quiz government minister Lord Parkinson on plans for an additional bank holiday to mark the Queen's Platinum Jubilee in 2022, from Monday 16 November

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Coverage of the International Trade Committee session with evidence from Secretary of State Liz Truss, from Wednesday 4 November.

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: What is the point of the world's nuclear watchdog the International Atomic Energy Agency? Its task is to ensure that countries intent on developing nuclear power don't use their programmes as cover for development of weapons of mass destruction. But is the task impossible? HARDtalk's Stephen Sackur speaks to the new IAEA chief, Rafael Grossi. From the continued bitter arguments over Iran, to North Korea, and Saudi Arabia, is the IAEA another example of a global agency undermined by geopolitical division?

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Coverage of the backbench business debate in the House of Commons on International Men's Day, from Thursday 19 November

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: In his final lecture, Mark Carney turns his attention to climate change, arguing that the roots of our environmental emergency lie in a deeper crisis of values

programme.desc: Coverage of the joint session of the Science and Technology committee and the Health and Social Care committee looking at the performance of NHS Test and Trace with evidence from the Executive Chair, Dido Harding, and its Chief Medical Adviser, Dr Susan Hopkins.

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Scientists have discovered water on the sunlit surface of the moon for the first time, but does it matter? The moon is back in vogue in terms of space exploration - the US says it will put astronauts back on the lunar surface by 2024. It's supposed to be the precursor to a manned mission to Mars. Stephen Sackur speaks to Jacob Bleacher, chief exploration scientist at Nasa. In this time of pandemic and climate change here on Earth, is space exploration a potential lifeline or a massive vanity project?

programme.desc: Part two of two. The Wolf puts a sharp twist into some of the stories children are most fond of by telling them in his own pointed way

programme.title: Ronia, Nighean a' Mheirlich/Ronja, the Robber's Daughter
programme.desc: Ronia, the robber’s daughter, is born on a stormy night when lightning strikes and divides the fort into two halves

programme.title: Ronia, Nighean a' Mheirlich/Ronja, the Robber's Daughter

programme.title: A' Bhliadhna 2020
programme.desc: Anne Lundon looks back on the main news stories of 2020, including a series of special reports about the impact of the coronavirus, and a quick whirl through other topics

programme.title: Alleluia! (Spiritual Music & Verse)
programme.desc: Iain MacKinnon presents a mix of hymns, psalms and faith stories. Plus, Mairi MacInnes sings Cliu an Uain, James Graham performs Psalm 8, Mairi MacInnes shares the role faith plays in her life and Angela MacEachen reads from the Book of Proverbs

programme.title: Iorram - Boat Song
programme.desc: A cinema documentary entirely in Scottish Gaelic, Iorram (Boat Song) is a lyrical portrait of life in Scotland's Outer Hebrides, past and present. Director Alastair Cole takes the audience on an immersive journey into the heart and soul of a 1000-year-old community, blending archive sound recordings of voices from the past with visuals of island life today and an original score by acclaimed folk musician Aidan O'Rourke. At the core of the film is an extraordinary trove of sound archive, recorded by pioneering Scottish ethnographers in the mid-20th century, who visited the Western Isles to capture the hardship and romance of life lived in precarious balance with the sea. These newly restored recordings preserve an oral history of lore and legends, tall tales and tragedies, passed down through generations of Gaelic speakers. This soundtrack of voices, stories and songs from the past is accompanied by moving imagery of the daily working rhythm of the islands, on land and on water, shot ov

programme.title: Dystonia: Beatha air Fhiaradh
programme.desc: For almost 40 years, DK MacPhee has lived with dystonia, which leaves him unable to straighten his head. DK gives an insight into the impact this has on his daily life and his ongoing battle to find treatment or relief. As his condition has deteriorated, with his head falling further to one side, the more his life has been affected. A number of years ago, he had to give up playing football, weekly, with his friends, something which he very much misses and he is determined to try and play again. Will this be possible?

programme.title: NY2SY
programme.desc: In a focused and candid delivery, Niall Iain Macdonald recounts his adventure story, attempting to conquer the might of the Atlantic Ocean in a solo rowing boat. Supported by footage taken over several years, both ashore and at sea, he shares the dramatic highs and the extreme lows of his 2018 adventure. In the lead-up to his latest attempt he records his slow, steady progress, the cancellations, postponements, accidents and frustrations that met him along the way, and also the sheer elation of finally setting out to sea. Illustrated with unique footage captured on board as he rowed

programme.title: Twin
programme.desc: Erik confronts Ingrid over Adam's dark side and is surprised to discover that his brother's life was far from perfect. Karin struggles at school, but when Erik tries to play the role of a dedicated father, he only reinforces her suspicion that something is seriously wrong and she decides to take matters into her own hands. In Norwegian

programme.title: Mighty Mike

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mr. Bean accidentally spills all of Mrs. Wicket's new, expensive perfume on the floor. Can he hide the crime and cover his tracks?

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mr Bean is on his way to the seaside when the mini runs out of petrol. Undeterred he manages to get a push, but for some reason the do-gooders don't want to push him all the way to the beach!

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: Mavis wins employee of the month and the right to share breakfast with Aunt Lydia

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: Dr Gillman saves Wendy's ponytail and now she's indebted to him

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: The Spuds don't care about a carbon footprint, since George has come home with a haul of free electronics that they all want to test out.

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: Dave wants to learn to play the drums at school, but is given the recorder. With a talent show around the corner, Dave gets practising.

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: In East Sussex, the expert walker sets off from the village of East Dean, as she explores one of the longest undeveloped coastlines on the south coast - one that is disappearing at an erosion rate of almost one meter a year. During her three-hour walk Julia discovers the area's rich social history at seaside settlement Birling Gap, learns of the mystery of Sherlock Holmes' retirement, and hears how a lighthouse had to be shifted 17 metres back from the cliff edge

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh kick-starts the year with joyful January plants and new craft drinks, and welcomes Father Brown's Nancy Carroll and presenter Kirsty Gallacher

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner offer tips on what to do with Christmas leftovers. They prepare leek and potato soup served with cheese toastie fingers, vegetarian thatched cottage pies, and sausage orzo served with pangritata. For dessert, they whip up some mini fruit crumbles, using leftover shortbread

programme.title: Peter Pan (2003)
programme.desc: The adventures of the boy who never grows old, as he whisks Wendy Darling and her brothers away to the magical world of Neverland - where they come face to face with fearsome Captain Hook. Adaptation of JM Barrie's fantasy novel, starring Jeremy Sumpter, Rachel Hurd-Wood, Jason Isaacs and Lynn Redgrave

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts as MasterChef judge Gregg Wallace, comedian Chris Ramsey and model and presenter Vogue Williams take part in the game show, in which they must answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping counters down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - with the highest scorer playing for the £20,000 jackpot for their chosen charity

programme.title: Lingo
programme.desc: Adil Ray invites three pairs of contestants to call upon their powers of deduction in a battle of words. Using their powers of deduction, they must correctly and quickly work out the words that appear in the Lingo grids and Puzzlewords. The last remaining duo have the opportunity to win the money they've banked earlier in the show, potentially doubling their winnings or leaving with nothing. First up are father and son Simon and Will, competition fanatic Sue and husband Paul, and mother and daughter Dawn and Regan

programme.title: Fantastic Beasts: The Crimes of Grindelwald (2018)
programme.desc: In an attempt to scupper an evil sorcerer's plans of raising pure-blood wizards to rule over all non-magical beings, Albus Dumbledore enlists his former student Newt Scamander, who agrees to help. Second part of JK Rowling's Harry Potter prequel saga, starring Eddie Redmayne, Katherine Waterston and Jude Law

programme.title: Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Gino D'Acampo hosts this revamped version of the game show in which two families guess the answers to surveys of 100 people in a bid to play for the £30,000 jackpot. This time, it's the turn of the Craig family from Falkirk, who take on the Mills family from Essex

programme.title: Beat the Chasers
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh returns to host the spin-off from The Chase in which contestants take on two, three, four or all five of the quiz's resident experts - Jenny 'The Vixen' Ryan, Anne 'The Governess' Hegerty, Shaun 'The Dark Destroyer' Wallace, Mark 'The Beast' Labbett and Paul 'The Sinnerman' Sinha, hoping to win big money against the odds

programme.title: And Finally - With Trevor McDonald
programme.desc: The presenter's favourite trademark ‘And Finally' news stories from across his 30-year career at ITN, which offered a glimpse into the nation's lighter, quirkier moments. He catches up with some of the protagonists, including the man who turned his lounge into the Starship Enterprise, and the girl who was christened with 217 names

programme.title: Billy Connolly: It's Been a Pleasure

programme.desc: Thinking he's fixing Weasel's coffee maker, Magoo tweaks Fizz's teleporter, the invention that the hamster plans to exhibit at the innovation tradeshow.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: While helping Linda clean her attic, Magoo gets shrunken and teleported into Fizz's brain. Time for a spring clean.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo takes Fizz for a toy containing top secret information, which he steals from a secret agent, but it's actually just weasel.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: An incident at an orbiting space hub calls for the staff of International Rescue to give their operation a massive overhaul, one that will make Thunderbird Two capable of venturing into space

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Mike is delighted when Iris invites him over for a cup of tea on the terrace, but with the raccoons to contend with, will he make it to his date on time

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: While Mike is distracted by flirting with Iris, the raccoons sneak into the house

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: If only Mike were a little bit more muscular, Iris would definitely look at him in a new light. At least that's what he tells himself when he sees Stephan's new workout bench. Unfortunately, it's not that easy

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mr. Bean accidentally spills all of Mrs. Wicket's new, expensive perfume on the floor. Can he hide the crime and cover his tracks?

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mr Bean is on his way to the seaside when the mini runs out of petrol. Undeterred he manages to get a push, but for some reason the do-gooders don't want to push him all the way to the beach!

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: Mavis wins employee of the month and the right to share breakfast with Aunt Lydia

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: Dr Gillman saves Wendy's ponytail and now she's indebted to him

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: The Spuds don't care about a carbon footprint, since George has come home with a haul of free electronics that they all want to test out.

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: Dave wants to learn to play the drums at school, but is given the recorder. With a talent show around the corner, Dave gets practising.

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: In East Sussex, the expert walker sets off from the village of East Dean, as she explores one of the longest undeveloped coastlines on the south coast - one that is disappearing at an erosion rate of almost one meter a year. During her three-hour walk Julia discovers the area's rich social history at seaside settlement Birling Gap, learns of the mystery of Sherlock Holmes' retirement, and hears how a lighthouse had to be shifted 17 metres back from the cliff edge

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh kick-starts the year with joyful January plants and new craft drinks, and welcomes Father Brown's Nancy Carroll and presenter Kirsty Gallacher

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner offer tips on what to do with Christmas leftovers. They prepare leek and potato soup served with cheese toastie fingers, vegetarian thatched cottage pies, and sausage orzo served with pangritata. For dessert, they whip up some mini fruit crumbles, using leftover shortbread

programme.title: Peter Pan (2003)
programme.desc: The adventures of the boy who never grows old, as he whisks Wendy Darling and her brothers away to the magical world of Neverland - where they come face to face with fearsome Captain Hook. Adaptation of JM Barrie's fantasy novel, starring Jeremy Sumpter, Rachel Hurd-Wood, Jason Isaacs and Lynn Redgrave

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts as MasterChef judge Gregg Wallace, comedian Chris Ramsey and model and presenter Vogue Williams take part in the game show, in which they must answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping counters down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - with the highest scorer playing for the £20,000 jackpot for their chosen charity

programme.title: Lingo
programme.desc: Adil Ray invites three pairs of contestants to call upon their powers of deduction in a battle of words. Using their powers of deduction, they must correctly and quickly work out the words that appear in the Lingo grids and Puzzlewords. The last remaining duo have the opportunity to win the money they've banked earlier in the show, potentially doubling their winnings or leaving with nothing. First up are father and son Simon and Will, competition fanatic Sue and husband Paul, and mother and daughter Dawn and Regan

programme.title: Fantastic Beasts: The Crimes of Grindelwald (2018)
programme.desc: In an attempt to scupper an evil sorcerer's plans of raising pure-blood wizards to rule over all non-magical beings, Albus Dumbledore enlists his former student Newt Scamander, who agrees to help. Second part of JK Rowling's Harry Potter prequel saga, starring Eddie Redmayne, Katherine Waterston and Jude Law

programme.title: Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Gino D'Acampo hosts this revamped version of the game show in which two families guess the answers to surveys of 100 people in a bid to play for the £30,000 jackpot. This time, it's the turn of the Craig family from Falkirk, who take on the Mills family from Essex

programme.title: Beat the Chasers

programme.title: And Finally - With Trevor McDonald

programme.title: Billy Connolly: It's Been a Pleasure

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Bobby Briggs, the criminal who paralysed Joe years before, breaks out of jail - but he does not bargain on the community's hostile reception, as Quagmire, Joe and Peter unite to hunt him down. Meanwhile, Brian grows jealous when Lois gets a cat to look after

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Roger meets Meredith, the most interesting woman in Langley Falls, and he struggles to create a new, more fabulous persona to keep up with her. When he fails, Francine suggests the dejected alien embark on a journey of self-discovery, which doesn't go exactly as planned. Meanwhile, Stan and the others overdo it during a workout, and end up feeling too sore to move, giving Klaus the chance to throw himself a party

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Roger talks Francine into taking advantage of Stan's absence by playing the 'Widow Game', which involves the two of them posing as widows in order to gain sympathy - and free stuff. However, the fun is spoiled when Francine begins to seriously contemplate how she would survive if Stan really were to die. Meanwhile, Steve and his friends stage wrestling matches in the basement, but things get out of hand when an uninvited participant shows up

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland goes on a hunting trip against Donna's wishes, and becomes hard of hearing after having an accident. programme.desc: Cleveland works overtime to save up enough money to take the family on a cruise, and begins taking energy pills to stay awake - but soon becomes addicted to them and is forced to go to rehab. With the voice of Jason Sudeikis (Horrible Bosses)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: The council gives a ruling on Ray's development, Grace turns up on Michael's doorstep, and Evelyn regrets her harsh words to Arthur

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: The residents reel from their eviction notices, Arthur offers Evelyn the chance of a new life, and Michael and Grace take a paternity test

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Three singletons go up against each other in the ultimate shopping challenge, as they try to win the affections of footballer Roger by each buying him an outfit

programme.title: Love Bites
programme.desc: Scarlett Moffatt hosts a dating series in which three suitors cook a three-course meal, all using the same box of ingredients, with the best earning a date with a singleton.

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: The council gives a ruling on Ray's development, Grace turns up on Michael's doorstep, and Evelyn regrets her harsh words to Arthur
